CN223283057U.title: Upper boiler barrel of boiler
CN223283057U.abstract: The upper boiler barrel comprises a barrel body, a front sealing head and a rear sealing head, an underwater pore plate parallel to an axis is arranged in the barrel body along the lower position of the axis, the left side and the right side of the underwater pore plate are respectively connected with a baffle which inclines upwards, the upper ends of the baffles are fixedly connected with the inner wall of the barrel body, and the lower ends of the baffles are fixedly connected with the lower end of the barrel body. Through holes are formed in positions, corresponding to the left and right baffles, of the barrel and are connected with the ascending pipe; a sealing plate is arranged at the front ends of the underwater pore plate and the two baffles, the lower edge of the sealing plate is lower than the underwater pore plate, a pore plate parallel to the underwater pore plate is arranged between the lower edge of the sealing plate and the front sealing head, and a through hole is formed in the position, corresponding to the pore plate, of the cylinder body and connected with a downcomer. According to the technical scheme, it can be ensured that a water-steam mixture entering from the ascending pipe can only flow into the lower half part of the cavity of the barrel firstly, then steam penetrates through the underwater pore plate and enters the upper half part of the cavity of the barrel upwards, first-time steam-water separation is achieved, and the water inlet of the descending pipe can be prevented from forming vortexes.

CN120586111A.title: Intelligent temperature control instantaneous sterilization and rapid cooling equipment
CN120586111A.abstract: The invention provides intelligent temperature control instantaneous sterilization and rapid cooling equipment. The intelligent temperature control instantaneous sterilization and rapid cooling equipment comprises a bottom plate, a shell is arranged at the upper end of the bottom plate, a water drainage pipe is arranged on one side of the shell, a water steam drainage hole is formed in the upper end of the shell, an inner container is arranged in the shell, and the outer surface of the inner container is in threaded connection with a pressurization top cover; an electric telescopic rod is fixedly connected to the top inner wall of the pressurizing top cover, a pressing plate is fixedly connected to the lower end of the electric telescopic rod, a second water inlet pipe is fixedly connected to the inner wall of the shell, a cooling-water machine is fixedly connected to the upper end of the bottom plate, and a water pump is installed in a water tank of the cooling-water machine; the outer surface of the inner container is fixedly connected with a heating resistance ring, a sealing heat insulation sleeve is arranged outside the inner container, and through the structure, efficient sterilization can be achieved, cooling can be conducted in time, the humidity in the inner container can be reduced in time, and adverse effects of the high-humidity environment on the inner container and objects in the inner container are prevented.

CN223283054U.title: Hot water and steam multipurpose electric heating furnace
CN223283054U.abstract: The utility model discloses a hot water steam multipurpose electric heating furnace which comprises an electric heater and a furnace body, and further comprises a cold water collecting pipe, a hot water collecting pipe and at least one coil pipe, water and cold water are stored in an inner cavity of the furnace body, the hot water collecting pipe is in a barrel shape and located above the cold water collecting pipe, a pipe body of the cold water collecting pipe is arranged in the inner cavity of the furnace body, and the coil pipe is arranged in the inner cavity of the furnace body. A cold water inlet end of the cold water collecting pipe is arranged on the outer side of the furnace body, a pipe body of the hot water collecting pipe is arranged in an inner cavity of the furnace body, a hot water outlet end of the hot water collecting pipe is arranged on the outer side of the furnace body, the coil pipe is vertically arranged in the inner cavity of the furnace body in a spiral shape, and a pipe opening in the lower end of the coil pipe is communicated with an inner cavity of the cold water collecting pipe. A pipe opening in the upper end of the coil pipe is communicated with an inner cavity of the hot water collecting pipe, the boiler body is vertically arranged, and a steam outlet is formed in the top end of the boiler body. The multi-heat-source heat supply system can provide hot water media and steam media, the two heat supply media are independent of each other, and the multi-heat-source heat supply requirement of a user is met.

CN223214649U.title: Secondary water supply and storage device
CN223214649U.abstract: The utility model relates to the technical field of secondary water supply, in particular to a secondary water supply and storage device which is characterized in that a piston assembly is arranged in a water storage tank to separate air from water, the piston assembly is composed of a positioning plate, a pressing plate and a positioning ring, and the water-steam separation effect is ensured through deformation of the positioning ring and sealing of a rubber pad; the filter assembly and the baffle on the air inlet pipe further guarantee the purity of air and reduce entering of water vapor, the energy accumulator on the water inlet and outlet pipe enhances the system stability, and the technical effect of improving the water quality and the water supply stability of the secondary water supply and storage device is achieved.

CN223262777U.title: Frying and baking machine
CN223262777U.abstract: The utility model relates to a frying and baking machine which comprises a base assembly, an upper cover assembly and a water tank assembly, the upper cover assembly can cover the base assembly, the base assembly comprises a lower baking tray, the lower baking tray is provided with a cooking groove and a water groove, the water tank assembly is installed on the base assembly or the upper cover assembly, the water tank assembly comprises a box body and a pipeline, and the box body can be communicated with the water groove through the pipeline. A switch valve is arranged on the pipeline, the switch valve comprises a valve body and a valve element rotatably arranged in the valve body, and the switch valve controls the pipeline to be opened and closed through rotation of the valve element. According to the frying and baking machine, the water tank does not need to be filled with water before cooking, only the water needs to be added into the box body, only the valve element needs to be rotated to control opening and closing of the switch valve in the cooking process, then the box body can be controlled to add the water into the water tank or stop adding the water, steam can be rapidly generated, and the upper cover assembly does not need to be opened; therefore, the problem that the cooking effect is affected due to steam escape and rapid temperature reduction caused by uncovering and water adding is avoided.

CN120557922A.title: Drying, sterilizing and heating cabinet for medicine bags
CN120557922A.abstract: The medicine bag drying, sterilizing and heating cabinet comprises a water storage tank and a heating part, and the water storage tank is provided with a steam channel and a water return channel; a plurality of disinfection boxes are sequentially arranged above the water storage tank, and each disinfection box is provided with an air inlet channel and an air outlet channel; a water-vapor separation part and a condensation box are arranged between the water storage tank and the disinfection box; the water-steam separation part is communicated between the steam channel and the air inlet channel; the water-vapor separation part is provided with a drainage channel which is communicated with the water return channel; the condensing box communicates with the air outlet channel and the water return channel; the condensation boxes are arranged on one sides of the corresponding disinfection boxes and are sequentially stacked above the water storage box, and the upper condensation box is communicated with the lower condensation box through a drainage pipe. The water storage tank is heated through the heating part to produce high-temperature and high-humidity steam, water in the steam is separated through the water-steam separation part, the high-temperature and low-humidity steam is obtained to dry and heat a medicine bag, the water is recycled to the water storage tank through the condensation box, and the medicine bag drying and heating device has the advantages of being small in consumption and high in safety.

CN120531105A.title: Preparation method of ready-to-eat cooked red date product
CN120531105A.abstract: The invention relates to a preparation method of an instant cooked red date product. The method comprises the following steps: putting a red date raw material into an electrolytic bath with tap water to be electrolyzed and rinsed for 1-10 minutes; steaming the red dates obtained by electrolytic rinsing; then drying the steamed red dates with hot air; and finally, putting the hot-air-dried red dates into a microwave drying machine for microwave drying and sterilization treatment to obtain the instant cooked red date product. The total number of bacterial colonies and the number of coliforms and molds in the ready-to-eat red date cooked product are effectively reduced through combination of alkaline electrolyzed water, steam steaming, hot air drying and microwave treatment, and the stability and safety of the eating quality of the ready-to-eat red date cooked product can be guaranteed without other preservatives; the time required by microwave treatment can be greatly shortened, and the eating quality loss of the instant red date cooked product is reduced; the water vapor steaming process changes the tissue structure of the red date pulp, and brings unique texture and taste; according to the invention, the eating quality and safety of the ready-to-eat cooked red date product are comprehensively improved.

CN120402884A.title: Compression fire hot standby thermodynamic system coupled with solar heat storage and operation method
CN120402884A.abstract: The invention provides a pressing fire hot standby thermodynamic system coupled with solar heat storage and an operation method, and belongs to the field of circulating fluidized bed unit equipment. The system is provided with a solar thermal collector, a high/low-temperature fused salt tank, a fused salt-water heat exchanger and a fused salt-steam heat exchanger, wherein a fused salt outlet of the solar thermal collector, the high-temperature fused salt tank, a high-temperature fused salt pump, an eighth three-way valve, the fused salt-water heat exchanger, a seventh three-way valve, a low-temperature fused salt tank, a low-temperature fused salt pump and a fused salt inlet of the solar thermal collector are communicated in sequence; a second outlet of the eighth three-way valve communicates with a fused salt side inlet of the fused salt-steam heat exchanger, and a fused salt side outlet of the fused salt-steam heat exchanger communicates with a second inlet of the seventh three-way valve. During operation, solar energy is stored in fused salt of the high-temperature fused salt tank in a conventional state; when fire pressing hot standby is started, energy in the fused salt passes through the heat exchanger to heat feed water/steam to generate steam meeting the requirement for safe operation of the steam turbine. According to the method, the banking-up period is prolonged, and the deep peak regulation capacity is improved.

HRUM20250014U2.title: STEAM GENERATOR FOR STERILIZATION OF WOOL
HRUM20250014U2.abstract: The steam in this steam generator for wool sterilisation is most often produced in boilers heated by natural gas or electricity. The steam is distributed through steel pipes, and the operating pressure of the steam at the outlet is regulated as necessary by a pressure regulator. The water in the boiler is chemically treated as necessary to control pH values, mineral deposition and foam formation. Efficiency tests of the use of water steam for sheep wool sterilisation have shown that for optimal steam penetration and effective sterilisation, it is necessary to use steam that must be dry, so that it contains 97% steam (water vapour) and 3% water. In the case of wet steam, that is, one that has less than 97% water vapour, it has been observed that problems of poor heat transfer occur, problems in the process, such as "wet packages", a long-term decrease in system efficiency occurs, and the costs of system maintenance and operation increase.

CN223163034U.abstract: The utility model discloses a self-locking device for a hoisting buckle of a saline water injection barrel. WO2025136149A1.abstract: The group of inventions relates to the field of producing carbon materials which are modified by silicon-containing compounds and exhibit enhanced hydrophobicity for the manufacture of low-density carbonaceous sorbents for removing hydrocarbons from the surface of water, or for the manufacture of graphite foil that exhibits enhanced sealing ability with respect to water, steam and aqueous media. A method for manufacturing a hydrophobic material based on thermally expanded graphite includes the steps of: saturating intercalated graphite with a composition containing at least one organosilicon compound selected from alkyl-substituted and phenyl-substituted silanes and/or polyalkyl and polyphenyl siloxanes, an aqueous solution of ammonia and an organic solvent, where the ratio of the mass of intercalated graphite to the mass of the saturating composition is equal to 1:(0.02-5); removing the excess liquid phase; drying the mixture to a free-flowing state; and heat treating the dried mixture at a temperature of from 300 to 1000°С. The invention makes it possible to produce particles of thermally expanded graphite with improved hydrophobic characteristics.

CN223111486U.title: Water vapor separation device
CN223111486U.abstract: The utility model discloses a water vapor separation device in the technical field of drinking water equipment, which comprises a box body with a cavity inside; a water inlet pipe; the water outlet pipe comprises an upper pipe section, a transition section and a lower pipe section which are sequentially connected from top to bottom and are coaxially arranged; two partition plates; a convergence sleeve; the water retaining block is arranged on the inner top surface of the separation area and located on the left sides of the two partition plates, the periphery of the water retaining block is matched with the inner side surface of the separation area to form a split-flow steam channel, and the bottom surface of the water retaining block is matched with the inner low surface of the separation area to form a split-flow water channel; according to the utility model, the situation of remixing caused by large-area contact of separated hot water and steam is avoided, continuous separation in the flowing process can be realized, the water-steam separation effect is improved, the flowing is stable, and the phenomenon of water bifurcation caused by water flow splashing to flow out from the exhaust port is avoided; therefore, the buffer area is arranged before water is discharged, so that the impact force and the flow speed of the discharged water are reduced, and the water is taken more stably and smoothly.

CN120331905A.title: Heating equipment
CN120331905A.abstract: The invention relates to heating equipment. The equipment comprises a first generator set, a second generator set and a heating device, the first generator set is in a high-back-pressure working condition, and the second generator set is in a low-pressure cylinder cutting working condition; the exhaust end of the first generator set communicates with a water inlet of the heating equipment and the water inlet end of the heating device, and the water outlet end of the heating device communicates with the exhaust end of the second generator set and a water outlet of the heating equipment. Inlet water flows into the heating device through the water inlet, steam generated by the first generator set in the power generation process flows into the heating device through the exhaust end of the first generator set, and the inlet water and the steam generated by the first generator set in the power generation process are heated in the heating device to form heated water. Steam generated in the power generation process of the second generator set exchanges heat with heating water through the exhaust end of the second generator set to form heating water, and the heating water flows out through the water outlet. By adopting the method, the heating effect and the power generation income of the heating equipment can be ensured.

UA162222U.title: METHOD FOR INCREASING GEOMETRIC DIMENSIONS AND TEMPERATURE OF PYROLYSIS GAS COMBUSTION MASS IN SOLID FUEL HEATING DEVICES
UA162222U.abstract: Method for increasing geometric dimensions and temperature of pyrolysis gas combustion mass in solid fuel heating devices, in which solid heterogeneous carbonaceous raw materials-waste are compacted by loading it into a heating chamber that has an adjustable connection to the combustion chamber by at least one main line. Solid heterogeneous carbonaceous raw materials are heated in the heating chamber. Pyrolysis gases are released from solid heterogeneous carbonaceous raw materials-waste by heating, pyrolysis gases are moved to the combustion chamber, where they are burned, excess heat and exhaust gases are removed into the ambient air through the exhaust line. Additionally, during the combustion of pyrolysis gases in the combustion chamber, the air is rotated counterclockwise relative to the vertical by a vortex to the horizon and heated in the range from 600 to 900 °C. The combustion array is magnetized from the outside by a magnetic field with a magnetic induction Br of 1.45 to 1.48 T and magnetic energy BH of 398 to 422 kJ/m3, and water is fed into the heating chamber from the outside at a rate and volume of 1 to 10 drops per second. The water inside the heating chamber is heated to the state of superheated water vapor. The superheated water vapor is circulated with heated air. The superheated water vapor is fed into the pyrolysis gas combustion array, increasing the geometric dimensions and temperature of the pyrolysis gas combustion array from 1600 to 1800 °C with superheated water vapor. The release of combustion gases is regulated depending on the intensity of the flame.

WO2026045815A1.title: STRUCTURE D'ENCAPSULATION POUR MODULE DE PUCE ET SON PROCÉDÉ D'ENCAPSULATION
WO2026045815A1.abstract: Provided in the present invention are a packaging structure for a chip module and a packaging method therefor. The packaging structure comprises a substrate, at least one filter chip, a separation film and a molding compound layer. The filter chip is disposed on the substrate, the front surface of the filter chip faces the substrate, and a first gap is formed between the filter chip and the substrate; the separation film covers the side wall and the back surface of the filter chip and part of the surface of the substrate, so as to encapsulate the first gap and form a sealed first cavity between the filter chip and the substrate, and a second gap is provided between the edge of the separation film and the edge of the substrate; and the molding compound layer covers the remaining surface of the substrate and the separation film. Due to the second gap between the edge of the separation film and the edge of the substrate, the separation film is wrapped by the molding compound layer without being exposed to the air. When the packaging structure for a chip module operates in a high-humidity environment, water vapor cannot penetrate into the first cavity by means of the separation film, thereby avoiding the problem of performance degradation or even failure of the filter chip.

US20260061385A1.title: MASS PRODUCTION APPARATUS FOR HIGH-PURITY LITHIUM SULFIDE
US20260061385A1.abstract: When producing lithium sulfide by a reaction between a lithium raw material and hydrogen sulfide, the reaction is performed under relatively mild conditions compared to the conventional technology, so frequent repairs or replacements due to corrosion and breakdown of reactors and piping are not required, thereby improving the economic efficiency of the process. Since unreacted hydrogen sulfide and a solvent from which moisture has been removed are reused, process costs are reduced so that economic feasibility in mass production is ensured. Furthermore, moisture and water vapor generated in a lithium sulfide production reaction are effectively removed to prevent a reverse reaction into lithium hydroxide and promote a forward reaction so that high-quality lithium sulfide can be produced with high purity and high yield. In addition, particle size may be controlled in the micrometer range without a separate crushing space or crushing stage, thereby providing excellent convenience and mass production.

US20260062588A1.abstract: A method is provided for producing a modified polysilazane-cured coating that densifies a coating made of a polysilazane compound in a short period of time. WO2026040596A1.title: PROCÉDÉ ET APPAREIL DE TRAITEMENT DE STRUCTURE D'EMPILEMENT DE PUCES
WO2026040596A1.abstract: Provided in the embodiments of the present application are a chip stacking structure processing method and apparatus. The method comprises: placing a chip stacking structure in a processing chamber; evacuating the processing chamber, such that the pressure in the processing chamber reaches a first negative pressure value; within a first time period, keeping the pressure in the processing chamber below the first negative pressure value, such that moisture in the chip stacking structure is evaporated into water vapor; and within a second time period, continuously introducing a drying fluid into the processing chamber, so as to dry the water vapor, wherein the second time period is longer than the first time period. The chip stacking structure processing method and apparatus in the present application can effectively dry a chip stacking structure.

WO2026034773A1.title: PROCÉDÉ DE PRÉPARATION DE SULFURE DE LITHIUM
WO2026034773A1.abstract: The present invention relates to a method for preparing lithium sulfide. According to the present invention, when lithium sulfide is prepared by a reaction between a lithium raw material and hydrogen sulfide, the reaction proceeds under relatively mild conditions compared with conventional technologies. Thus, repair or replacement due to corrosion and failure of reactors and various piping components is not frequently required, thereby improving the economic feasibility of the process. In addition, the unreacted hydrogen sulfide and solvent from which moisture has been removed can be reused, thereby reducing process costs and securing economic feasibility in mass production. Furthermore, moisture and water vapor generated by the lithium sulfide production reaction are effectively removed, thereby preventing the reverse reaction to lithium hydroxide and promoting the forward reaction to prepare high-quality lithium sulfide with high purity and high yield.

WO2026040863A1.abstract: Provided are a highly reflective glaze layer having a composite structure and a manufacturing method therefor, a back sheet glass, and a photovoltaic module. Said highly reflective glaze layer is provided on a glass substrate (3), and comprises a surface layer (1) and a reflective layer (2), the surface layer (1) covering the surface and side surfaces of the reflective layer (2), and the tail end of the surface layer (1) being in contact with the surface of the glass substrate (3). The surface layer (1) is a high-temperature bonding-phase accumulation layer, and the reflective layer (2) is a white filler accumulation layer. Said highly reflective glaze layer has a dense surface and a significantly layered structure, and thus can form multi-gradient reflection and has excellent reflection ability, so that gap light can be more fully recovered and utilized. In addition, the glaze layer further exhibits greatly improved water vapor insulation and electrical insulation properties and passes both a 96-hour PCT and a 192-hour PID test.

WO2026034775A1.title: PROCÉDÉ DE PRODUCTION DE SULFURE DE LITHIUM
WO2026034775A1.abstract: The present invention relates to a method for producing lithium sulfide. According to the present invention, when producing lithium sulfide by a reaction between a lithium source and hydrogen sulfide, the reaction is performed under relatively mild conditions compared to the prior art, and thus the economic efficiency of the process is improved since frequent repairs or replacements necessitated by corrosion and failure of a reactor and various piping are not required. In addition, since dehydrated, unreacted hydrogen sulfide and solvents are recycled, the process cost is reduced, and thus economic efficiency can be achieved in mass production. Furthermore, moisture and water vapor generated by the lithium sulfide production reaction are effectively removed to prevent a reverse reaction to lithium hydroxide and promote a forward reaction, thereby enabling the production of high-quality lithium sulfide with high purity and high yield. In addition, particle size can be adjusted at a µm level without a separate pulverization space or pulverization step, and thus excellent convenience and mass productivity are achieved.

US20260055557A1.abstract: The invention relates to a method for producing a molded fiber product (1), which comprises the following method steps: arranging in a chamber (5) a suction mold (2) with a porous wall (4) having a contour corresponding to the contour of the molded fiber product (1) to be produced,introducing a fiber material-air mixture (6) into the chamber (5), the fiber material being distributed in the air in the form of solid particles,suction of the fiber material-air mixture (6) through the porous wall (4) of the suction mold (2) and compacting the fiber material to form the molded fiber product (1) on the porous wall (4),removing the molded fiber product (1) from the suction mold (2) and from the chamber (5).The invention is based on the task of providing a technically simple method and a technically simple device enabling the rapid and energy-efficient production of biodegradable molded fiber products with particularly low waste.For this purpose, the fiber material consists mainly of cellulose fibers, wherein the fiber material is moist and/or water is added to the fiber material-air mixture in the form of droplets or water vapor.

US20260058172A1.title: HUMIDIFIER
US20260058172A1.abstract: A humidifier for humidifying a dry supply air stream via a humid exhaust air stream may include a housing, a membrane block with a plurality of membranes, and at least one baffle. The housing may include a supply air inlet, a supply air outlet, an exhaust air inlet, and/or an exhaust air outlet. The membranes may be impermeable to air and permeable to water vapor. The membranes may form a supply air path through which the supply air stream is flowable and an exhaust air path through which the exhaust air stream is flowable. The baffle may be arranged in the housing such that an air stream formed by the supply air stream and/or the exhaust air stream contacts the baffle. The baffle may be structured and arranged to provide homogenization of streaming through the membrane block in the supply air path and/or in the exhaust air path.

WO2026035320A1.title: SPECTROSCOPIE DE POLARISATION À EFFET STARK ET PROCÉDÉS
WO2026035320A1.abstract: A method of and system for monitoring the level of hydrogen peroxide gas in a system such as an isolation barrier. Gas is sampled from the system to a gas cell. The sampled gas may include hydrogen peroxide gas and water vapor. A laser beam from a laser source at a known polarization state is passed through the gas cell and the hydrogen peroxide gas and water vapor therein. An electrical field is created in the gas cell to reduce optical interference from water vapor while preserving the ability to more accurately measure the concentration of hydrogen peroxide gas in the gas cell. A change in the polarization state of the laser beam after passage through the gas cell is detected. Based on the detected change in the polarization state of the laser beam, the concentration of the hydrogen peroxide gas in the gas cell is determined.

WO2026034164A1.title: APPAREIL POUR AUGMENTER LA TENEUR EN GABA DE FEUILLES DE THÉ, PROCÉDÉ POUR AUGMENTER LA TENEUR EN GABA DE FEUILLES DE THÉ, ET PROCÉDÉ DE PRODUCTION DE THÉ GABA
WO2026034164A1.abstract: Provided are an apparatus for increasing the GABA content of tea leaves, a method for increasing the GABA content of tea leaves, and a method for producing a GABA tea, by which it becomes possible to increase the GABA content in a short time and with a simple apparatus configuration.　The apparatus for increasing the GABA content of tea leaves is provided with: a low-temperature treatment means including a first low-temperature water vapor treatment means or a first low-temperature water immersion treatment means in which the temperature of the surface treatment of the tea leaves is maintained at a first temperature falling within the temperature range of 40-60°C for a predetermined set time; and a kill-green treatment means for deactivating an oxidation enzyme in the tea leaves that have been treated by the low-temperature treatment means.

US20260043736A1.title: STARK EFFECT POLARIZATION SPECTROSCOPY AND METHODS
US20260043736A1.abstract: A method of and system for monitoring the level of hydrogen peroxide gas in a system such as an isolation barrier. Gas is sampled from the system to a gas cell. The sampled gas may include hydrogen peroxide gas and water vapor. A laser beam from a laser source at a known polarization state is passed through the gas cell and the hydrogen peroxide gas and water vapor therein. An electrical field is created in the gas cell to reduce optical interference from water vapor while preserving the ability to more accurately measure the concentration of hydrogen peroxide gas in the gas cell. A change in the polarization state of the laser beam after passage through the gas cell is detected. Based on the detected change in the polarization state of the laser beam, the concentration of the hydrogen peroxide gas in the gas cell is determined.

MX2025009540A.title: JOHNSON AMBIENT ENERGY CONVERTER
MX2025009540A.abstract: An environmental energy converter includes a housing having an upper portion and a lower portion. The lower housing portion has a hydrophobic material portion. The upper portion has a vent opening in fluid communication with the environment. The housing contains a hygroscopic mass within the lower housing portion that is in fluid communication with the hydrophobic material portion. An ion-electrode conducting membrane assembly is coupled to the housing to allow the passage of ionized water or water vapor through the ion-electrode conducting membrane and into contact with the hygroscopic solution. An air duct may be coupled to the housing to provide an airflow to the ion-electrode conducting membrane and/or to the hydrophobic material portion.

MX2025002635A.title: PAVING MEMBRANE
MX2025002635A.abstract: A paving membrane (1) includes a flexible plastic layer impermeable to water and water vapor (4) of the film-like plastic having a top side (5) and a bottom side (6) and an anchor layer (11) firmly attached to the bottom side (6) of the plastic layer (4). The plastic layer includes a plurality of buttons (7) present on the top side, each of the buttons forming a button cavity (8) on the bottom side. Water or water vapour discharge channels (9) are formed on the underside of the plastic layer (4). A maximum height (hmax) of the membrane (1) is at most about 5 mm, and at least 200 buttons per 10 cm2 are provided in areas having buttons (7).

WO2026031924A1.title: ÉCRAN D'AFFICHAGE ET SON PROCÉDÉ DE PRÉPARATION, ET APPAREIL D'AFFICHAGE
WO2026031924A1.abstract: Provided in the present application are a display panel and a preparation method therefor, and a display apparatus. The display panel comprises a display area and a non-display area located on at least one side of the display area. The display panel comprises a substrate, a driving circuit, an inorganic layer, an organic layer and hydrophobic particles, wherein the substrate is located in the display area and the non-display area; the driving circuit is located in the non-display area; the inorganic layer is located on the side of the driving circuit away from the substrate, is located at least in the non-display area, and covers the driving circuit; the organic layer is located on the side of the inorganic layer away from the substrate, and is located in the display area and the non-display area; the organic layer is provided with at least one groove running through the organic layer, and the groove is located in the non-display area; and the hydrophobic particles are located on the side of the driving circuit away from the substrate, and are at least some of the hydrophobic particles are filled in the groove. In this way, the speed of water vapor eroding a display panel can be slowed down, thereby prolonging the service life of the display panel.

US20260042684A1.title: DESALINATION AND CRYSTALLIZATION SYSTEM OPERATING WITHOUT BRINE
US20260042684A1.abstract: A zero liquid brine desalination and crystallization system includes a humidification dehumidification (HDH) desalination system and includes a crystallization system. The HDH system includes a humidifier configured to humidify a carrier gas using saline water, and a dehumidifier configured to dehumidify the carrier gas to obtain desalinated water. The crystallization system is configured to receive the saline water from the HDH desalination system and includes an evaporation chamber configured to produce salt crystals and water vapor from the saline water, and a condenser configured to condense the water vapor. Herein, the HDH desalination system and the crystallization system are connected at the condenser for heat exchange between the water vapor and at least one selected from the group consisting of the saline water and the carrier gas.

US20260041802A1.title: MODULAR HYDROXYL RADICAL GENERATION AND OZONE-BASED DISINFECTION SYSTEM
US20260041802A1.abstract: The present invention relates to modular ozone-based disinfection systems that generate hydroxyl radicals (·OH) from ozone and water vapor using a high-temperature catalyst module. The system includes a purification configuration with an air inlet, air circulation subsystem, airflow path, and air outlet, along with a water supply configuration feeding purified water to an electrolytic ozone generator. A catalyst module, such as a PTC heating element, heats ozone-containing air to a hydroxyl radical generation temperature, while a downstream hydroxyl augmentation and ozone reduction module converts residual ozone and conditions the airflow for safe discharge. Embodiments may include a replaceable water cartridge, resin tank, multiple water source options, humidity and purity sensors, and a safety interlock connected to a controller. The modular design supports portable, fixed, or refrigerated installations, enabling on-demand hydroxyl radical production with integrated control, safety, and discharge verification for advanced air and surface disinfection.

US20260042960A1.title: RED PHOSPHOR, PREPARATION METHOD THEREFOR, AND USE THEREOF
US20260042960A1.abstract: The present application discloses a red phosphor, and a preparation method and an application thereof and relates to the field of luminescent material technology. The chemical composition of the red phosphor is A2M(1-x)F6:xMn4+. The A is selected from at least one of alkali metal elements, and the M is selected from at least one of IVA group elements or a Ti element and a value range of the x is 0<x≤0.05. The red phosphor is granular; and a concentration of tetravalent manganese ions in a radial direction from a center of a particle of the red phosphor to a surface of the particle of the red phosphor decreases gradually. In the present application, the concentration of the activator Mn4+ in a radial direction from the center of the particle of the red phosphor to its surface is set to a descending gradient distribution to buffer the phosphor powder from the erosion of water vapor, thereby improving its anti-aging performance and prolonging the service life.

MX2025008752A.title: MULTILAYER TEXTILE ASSEMBLIES FOR REVERSIBLE WATER VAPOR ABSORPTION
MX2025008752A.abstract: Described herein are multilayer moisture management assemblies for reversible absorption of water vapor where the assemblies regulate relative humidity in microclimates proximate the assemblies. The moisture management assemblies absorb water vapor from air when the relative humidity level in the air is at or above a threshold level, thus retarding further elevation of the relative humidity. Articles incorporating the assemblies are also disclosed.

US20260034518A1.abstract: A system involves interleaving high aspect ratio, rod-like aldehyde-modified cellulose nanomaterials (ACN) between graphene oxide (GO) sheets and utilizing crosslinkers to create a dense, crosslinked network that is highly permeable and selective to a molecule of interest, especially water and water vapor, and does not delaminate in water. US20260038853A1.title: FUEL CELL SYSTEM AND CONTROL METHOD OF FUEL CELL SYSTEM
US20260038853A1.abstract: A control method of a fuel cell system according to the present disclosure uses a fuel cell system including a raw material supply system configured to supply a raw material, a water vapor supply system configured to supply water vapor to the raw material supply system, a fuel cell configured to generate electric energy from hydrogen generated from the raw material and an oxidizing agent, and a recycle gas system configured to circulate a recycle gas, which is at least a part of an anode off-gas discharged from an anode of the fuel cell, to the raw material supply system. MX2025007225A.title: PROCESO Y COMPOSICION PARA LA PRODUCCION DE CONCRETO POLIMERICO UTILIZANDO AGUA CON SAL COMO MEDIO DE REACCION.
MX2025007225A.abstract: The present invention relates to an innovative process and composition for the manufacture of polymeric concrete using a salt water solution as a reaction medium. This approach replaces the glycols derived from fossil fuels, achieving a significant reduction in production costs between 30% and 40% and promoting environmental sustainability by generating water vapor as a reusable byproduct and allowing the recovery of up to 90% of the salt used. The composition includes an unsaturated polyester resin obtained from recycled PET bottles, styrene monomer, granulates and recycled sand, in addition to advanced additives such as graphene oxide, carbon black, and calcium carbonate, which together optimize the mechanical properties of the concrete. The production process incorporates ultrasonic dispersion technologies to ensure a homogeneous mixture and efficient integration of nanometer materials, improving tensile strength, compression, and chemical agents. This polymeric concrete is characterized by its versatility and sustainability, being applicable in sectors such as road infrastructure, sustainable construction, industrial environments, coastal structures and advanced architectures. In addition, the formulation thereof allows applications in paints, waterproofing agents, composite materials, decorative moulds, repairs, electronics, synthetic fibres, 3D printing and consumer goods, highlighting the adaptability thereof to different technical and industrial needs.

WO2026029165A1.title: FILM MULTICOUCHE DÉPOSÉ EN PHASE VAPEUR, STRUCTURE MULTICOUCHE, MATÉRIAU D'EMBALLAGE, COMPOSITION DE RÉCUPÉRATION ET PROCÉDÉ DE RECYCLAGE
WO2026029165A1.abstract: The present invention provides: a vapor-deposited multilayer film which has high oxygen barrier properties and high water vapor barrier properties, and has sufficient oxygen barrier properties even after a bending treatment; and the like. Provided is a vapor-deposited multilayer film comprising: a multilayer film in which a layer (A), as the outermost layer, a layer (B), and a layer (C) are stacked in this order; and a vapor-deposited inorganic layer (I) stacked on the exposed surface side of the layer (A). The vapor-deposited inorganic layer (I) is a vapor-deposited metal layer that is mainly composed of aluminum, or a vapor-deposited inorganic oxide layer that is mainly composed of alumina or silica. The layer (A) contains, as a main component, an ethylene-vinyl alcohol copolymer (a) that has an ethylene unit content of 36-60 mol%; the layer (B) contains, as a main component, an ethylene-vinyl alcohol copolymer (b) that has an ethylene unit content of 20-35 mol%; and the layer (C) contains a polyethylene or a polypropylene as a main component. The average thickness of the multilayer film is 300 µm or less.

WO2026023732A1.title: DISPOSITIF D'ÉLIMINATION D'HUILE UTILISANT LA CHALEUR ET LA FORCE CENTRIFUGE
WO2026023732A1.abstract: This oil removal device using heat and centrifugal force can efficiently remove oil by using heat and centrifugal force, and thus can improve the taste and texture of fried food. In addition, a plurality of baskets formed to have step differences are used to prevent fried food from clumping together, and oil is removed while discharging oil vapor and water vapor through an air vent, thereby making it possible to minimize the reabsorption of oil separated from fried food into other fried food.

WO2026023538A1.title: MATÉRIAU D'EMBALLAGE EXTERNE POUR MATÉRIAU D'ISOLATION THERMIQUE SOUS VIDE, MATÉRIAU D'ISOLATION THERMIQUE SOUS VIDE, ARTICLE AVEC MATÉRIAU D'ISOLATION THERMIQUE SOUS VIDE, ET PROCÉDÉ DE PRODUCTION DE MATÉRIAU D'EMBALLAGE EXTERNE POUR MATÉRIAU D'ISOLATION THERMIQUE SOUS VIDE
WO2026023538A1.abstract: The present disclosure provides an outer packaging material for a vacuum heat-insulating material, the outer packaging material having a water vapor permeability of 0.1 g/(m2·day) or less and an oxygen permeability of 0.1 cc/(m2·day·atm) or less. The outer packaging material for a vacuum heat-insulating material has a plane-direction thermal diffusivity α and a thickness T which satisfy the following (expression 1). Expression 1: α≤6.375×10-5/(T×V) (In the expression, α is the plane-direction thermal diffusivity (m2/s) of the outer packaging material for a vacuum heat-insulating material, T is the thickness (m) of the outer packaging material for a vacuum heat-insulating material, and V is the volumetric specific heat of the outer packaging material for a vacuum heat-insulating material and is 2.2×106 J/(m3·K) to 2.4×106 J/(m3·K).)

US20260026974A1.title: MULTI-FUNCTIONAL ANALGESIC-RELEASING WOUND DRESSING
US20260026974A1.abstract: A wound dressing including a porous biosynthetic polymer film comprising a poly(lactide-co-caprolactone) (“PLC”) copolymer or other suitable polymer matrix, and a local analgesic loaded therein, to provide pain relief over an extended period of time (e.g., for 5-7 days), e.g., for healing of a partial thickness dermal injury. The PLC copolymer may have a particular lactide/caprolactone molar ratio (e.g., from 2:1 to 9:1) to ensure slow, sustained release of the bupivacaine or other analgesic. The dressing may provide a water vapor transmission rate from 1000 g/m2·day to 3000 g/m2·day to facilitate fast healing and regrowth of new epithelial tissue beneath the dressing. The dressing is configured to remain positioned over the dermal injury, providing wound protection for about 14 days, after which is simply debrides away, falls off, or is easily and painlessly removed, once new epithelial tissue regrows.

AU2025205300A1.title: Atmospheric correction
AU2025205300A1.abstract: This disclosure relates to machine learning models for performing atmospheric correction on an input image. To train a machine learning model to perform atmospheric correction on an input image comprising atmospheric distortion, a processor applies a first trained machine learning model to a training image to determine a first output image, the first trained machine learning model being configured to perform atmospheric correction on an input image comprising atmospheric distortion. The processor applies a second machine learning model to the training image to determine a second output image, wherein the second machine learning model has a smaller model architecture than the first trained machine learning model. The processor trains the second machine learning model by minimising a loss based on the first output image and the second output image to determine a second trained machine learning model. This disclosure relates to machine learning models for performing atmospheric correction on an input image. AU2024295884A1.title: MATERIALS, MANUFACTURING METHOD AND DEVICE FOR CONVERTING AMBIENT HUMIDITY TO ELECTRICAL ENERGY AND USES THEREOF
AU2024295884A1.abstract: The present disclosure relates to a modular converter for continuous conversion of water vapor into electricity comprising: a porous matrix comprising compressed metal oxide nanopowder, wherein the porous matrix, is covered from first side by the functional layer, which partially may infuse in the porous matrix, preferably an aluminum phosphate; a layer of carbon electrode, preferably a layer of graphite on the active layer, metal or carbon current collectors on carbon electrode layer and layer of metal Au, Ag, Al, Cu, Fe, Zn, Mg, Li, Ni, Co, Ti, Zr, Sn, Cr, Y, Nb, Mo, Rd, Pt, Cd, Hf, Ta, W, C or their alloys. Schottky contact on the second side of the converter; and metal or carbon current collectors, on the layer of Schottky contact, wherein the second surface of the converter is directly opposite the first surface of the converter.

US12523035B1.title: Water vapor permeable ribbed membranes and related systems and related methods
US12523035B1.abstract: Water vapor permeable ribbed membranes and related methods are provided. A water vapor permeable ribbed membrane has a top surface and a bottom surface opposite the top surface. The water vapor permeable ribbed membrane comprises a plurality of ribs located on at least the bottom surface. An adhesive layer is located on the plurality of ribs. When the adhesive layer is adhered to a top surface of another water vapor permeable ribbed membrane, the adhesive layer has a peel adhesion of at least 1.5 lb/in, as measured according to ASTM D3330 (Method B). The water vapor permeable ribbed membrane has a water vapor permeability of greater than 10 perms, as measured according to ASTM E96B.

KR102907001B1.title: Smart industrial boiler exhaust gas circulation loop formation control device and method for three-effect type smart industrial boiler exhaust gas circulation loop formation control device for fine dust reduction water vapor evaporation and latent heat recycling
KR102907001B1.abstract: 본 발명에서는 기존 산업용 보일러 배기가스내 수증기의 경우에, 백연 발생, 오염물질 확산 방행, 2차 입자상 물질 생성 촉진, 에어로졸을 활성화시키는 문제점과, 보일러 배기가스의 경우에, 대기 중에 냉각되면서 응축시 미세먼지(PM2.5)를 생성시키는데, 이는 지름이 2.5㎛(마이크로미터, 1㎛=100만분의 1m) 이하인 초미세먼지로서, 크기가 작아 호흡기를 통해 폐와 혈관까지 침투하여 건강에 심각한 영향을 미치기 때문에, 탄소배출권 시장에서 권고하고, 미국 EPA (μg/m³) 기준 연평균 12 μg/m³ 이하로 생성시킬 수 있는 보일러 배기가스용 미세먼지 저감장치개발이 시급한 문제점을 개선하고자, 하이브리드형 배기열 회수 모듈(100), 저온 배기열 회수 흡수탑모듈(200), 스마트 증류탑 순환루프 형성모듈(300), 시상수·응축수 탱크부(400), 스마트제어부(500)로 구성됨으로서, 하이브리드형 배기열 회수 모듈을 통해, 현열과 잠열을 동시 회수하는 하이브리드 방식으로 적용하여, 기존보다 30~50% 이상의 배기열 회수 효율을 향상시킬 수 있고, 배기열 회수량이 증가함에 따라 보일러 연료 소모량을 20% 이상 절감시킬 수 있으며, 난방뿐만 아니라 냉방, 온수 공급까지 동시에 가능하여, 에너지원 활용의 범위를 넓힐 수 있고, 배기 가스 중 포함된 미세먼지(입자상물질, PM)와 수증기 응축이 용이하여, 탄소배출권 시장에도 미세먼지 저감장치로서 경쟁력있게 적용시킬 수 있고, 저온 배기열 회수 흡수탑모듈이 구성됨으로서, 하이브리드형 배기열 회수 모듈과 연계하여 현열 + 잠열을 동시에 회수하는 방식을 적용함으로써, 기존보다 낮은 온도 20~40℃ 에서도 배기열을 회수시킬 수 있고, 흡수제 직접 분사로 노점온도 하강 및 배기열 손실 최소화시킬 수 있으며, 4단형 흡수탑 구조로 흡수 효율을 기존보다 80% 향상시킬 수 있고, 기존에는 흡수제의 재사용이 어려워 운영 비용이 높았으나, 본 발명에서는 흡수제 재생 시스템을 포함하여 흡수제의 지속적인 사용이 가능하므로 경제성이 뛰어난 효과가 있고, 스마트 증류탑 순환루프 형성모듈을 통해, 저온 배기열 회수 흡수탑모듈을 활용하여, 폐열을 회수하여 저농도 흡수제를 예열하고, 낮은 온도에서도 증발이 가능하도록 감압 분위기를 형성시켜, 기존 대비 기존 대비 30~50% 이상의 에너지 절감 효과를 기대할 수 있고, 감압 분위기 내에서 증발 촉진 및 증류 속도를 기존에 비해, 1.5배~2배 향상시킬 수 있으며, 농축 흡수제 냉각기와 농축 흡수제 가열기를 활용하여 적절한 온도로 순환시킴으로써, 흡수제의 성능 저하를 방지하고 흡수제의 사용 수명을 기존보다 1.5배~3배 길게 연장시킬 수 있고, 증발된 수증기의 응축수를 시상수·응축수 탱크부로 이동하여 재사용될 수 있어, 수자원 절약율을 기존에 비해 80% 향상시킬 수 있으며, 스마트제어부가 구성됨으로서, 하이브리드형 배기열 회수 제어모드를 통해, 배기가스의 현열(150~120°C)과 잠열(40~20°C)을 동시에 회수하여 실내 난방 및 온수 공급에 재활용시킬 수 있고,단순히 공기를 데우는 것이 아니라, 배기열을 최대한 활용하여 냉난방 시스템의 에너지 효율을 기존에 비해 80% 향상시킬 수 있으며, 산업용 가스보일러의 백연 제거와 동시에 미세먼지(여과성+응축성)를 80% 이상 제거시킬 수 있고, 보일러 배기가스에 포함된 수분 제거율을 80% 이상 제거시킬 수 있는 미세먼지 저감·수증기 증발·잠열 재활용 쓰리이펙트형 스마트 산업용 보일러 배기가스 순환루프 형성제어장치 및 방법을 제공하는데 그 목적이 있다.

WO2026020150A1.title: SYSTÈMES ET PROCÉDÉS DE DÉSHYDRATANT THERMIQUE SOLAIRE À HAUT RENDEMENT
WO2026020150A1.abstract: A water generation system includes a housing having a surface to collect solar radiation; a sorption layer comprising a hygroscopic material to capture water vapor from a process gas during a sorption mode and release water vapor to a regeneration fluid during a desorption mode; and a thermally insulative transparent layer disposed above or upstream of the hygroscopic material to allow solar radiation to pass therethrough to the hygroscopic material and reduce thermal conductance from the hygroscopic material to the external environment. Generating water includes allowing solar radiation to pass through a thermally insulative transparent layer to a hygroscopic material; directing a regeneration fluid in a regeneration flow path to collect heat from the thermally insulative transparent layer and transfer collected heat to the hygroscopic material to release water vapor to the regeneration fluid; and condensing, via a heat exchange assembly, water vapor from the regeneration fluid.

WO2026016265A1.title: REVÊTEMENT COMPOSITE HYDROPHOBE/RÉSISTANT À LA CORROSION RICHE EN CHROME, SON PROCÉDÉ DE PRÉPARATION ET SON UTILISATION
WO2026016265A1.abstract: A hydrophobic/chromium-rich corrosion-resistant composite coating, a preparation method therefor, and the use thereof, relating to the technical field of material surface coatings. The preparation method comprises: preheating a workpiece; applying a coating slurry to the surface of the preheated workpiece, and performing drying and curing and sintering treatment to obtain a chromium-rich corrosion-resistant coating on the surface of the workpiece, the coating slurry comprising a solid-phase component and a liquid-phase component, wherein the solid-phase component comprises chromium powder, nickel powder, iron powder and a rare earth compound, and the liquid-phase component comprises Al(H2PO4)3, water glass, an ammonium halide, polyvinyl alcohol and Cr2O3; and spray-coating a layer of a superhydrophobic material on the surface of the chromium-rich corrosion-resistant coating, and then performing curing treatment to obtain the hydrophobic/chromium-rich corrosion-resistant composite coating. A workpiece undergoes chromizing treatment and superhydrophobic treatment and thus possesses corrosion resistance, wear resistance and hydrophobic properties, so that water vapor in tubes cannot stay on the inner walls of the tubes, thereby preventing corrosion of the inner walls of the tubes, improving the comprehensive performance of the tubes, and prolonging the service life.

US20260015706A1.title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR APPLYING A LAYER ONTO A FLAT STEEL PRODUCT
US20260015706A1.abstract: A device for applying a layer (to a front and/or rear side of a flat steel product includes a zinc alloy melt bath (11) (ZnAl; ZnAlMg) with an input side and an exit side, a stripping nozzle device with at least one gas nozzle for blowing off the front or rear side of the flat steel product with gas, the stripping nozzle device being arranged in the area of the exit side, and a water vapor device which is configured to emit gaseous water vapor and to provide a controlled water vapor atmosphere in the close range of the front and/or rear side of the flat steel product. The controlled water vapor atmosphere has an absolute local humidity which is greater than 1 g/m3 and less than 300 g/m3, the absolute local humidity preferably being in the range of 2.71 g/m3 to 50 g/m3.

US20260015705A1.title: DEVICE AND METHOD AND DEVICE FOR MOISTURE-CONTROLLED BLOW-OFF AFTER THE APPLICATION OF A LAYER ONTO A FLAT STEEL PRODUCT

WO2026011792A1.title: MOULE D'ESTAMPAGE À CHAUD DE PETIT LOT BASÉ SUR UN REFROIDISSEMENT PAR L'AIR DIRECTEMENT SUR LA SURFACE , ET PROCÉDÉ DE REFROIDISSEMENT
WO2026011792A1.abstract: A small-batch hot stamping mold based on direct surface air cooling, comprising a male mold (20) and a female mold (30), wherein a recess matching a punch of the male mold is provided on the female mold, a first cavity (21) recessed toward the interior of the male mold is provided on a surface of the male mold, a second cavity (31) recessed toward the interior of the female mold is further provided on a surface of the female mold, and the first cavity and the second cavity are configured to form an airflow cooling channel in communication with an external pipe; and water vapor introduced through the external pipe enters the mold, and circulates and exchanges heat in the airflow cooling channel formed by the first cavity and the second cavity and on the surface of the mold, so as to take away the heat from a stamped part and the surface of the mold. Further disclosed are a cooling method for a stamped part based on direct surface air cooling and a cooling method for a stamping mold based on direct surface air cooling. The stamping mold is provided with the cavities on the surface of the mold, the manufacturing cost and maintenance cost of the mold are low, and the production cost of a single piece is reduced.

US20260008689A1.abstract: An installation for the conversion of uranium hexafluoride (UF6) to uranium dioxide (UO2) comprises a hydrolysis reactor (4) for the conversion of UF6 into uranium oxyfluoride powder (UO2F2), a pyrohydrolysis furnace (6) for converting the UO2F2 powder supplied by the reactor (4) into UO2 powder, a supply device (8) comprising reagent injection ducts (10) for the injection of UF6, water vapor or H2, and a control system (16) designed to control the supply device (8) so as to supply at least one of the reagent injection ducts (10) with a neutral gas during a shut-down or start-up phase of the conversion installation.

US20260008700A1.title: ADIABATIC WATER DESALINATION SYSTEM
US20260008700A1.abstract: Methods and systems are disclosed for easy and cost-effective adiabatic water desalination, producing freshwater and/or potable water from saline sources with minimal energy input. This is achieved by utilizing adiabatic thermodynamic processes to reduce energy consumption compared to conventional methods. The disclosed methods and systems offer sustainable and cost-effective solutions to address freshwater scarcity. In an example of these methods, a saltwater tank with two chambers is rocked back and forth around an axis such that, during each half cycle of rocking, the vapor volume of one chamber expands and the vapor volume of the other chamber shrinks. The expansion of the chambers' vapor volume vaporizes the salt water, and the shrinkage of the chambers' vapor volume causes condensation of the water vapor, which will be collected in a separate tank.

US20260008695A1.title: METHOD FOR PRODUCING POSITIVE ELECTRODE ACTIVE MATERIAL
US20260008695A1.abstract: A method for producing a positive electrode active material comprising: (1) mixing an activation agent containing one or more types of alkali metal compounds with an electrode material mixture; (2) heating the mixture at a temperature at a melting start temperature of the activation agent or more; (3) bringing the mixture after step (2) into contact with a water-containing liquid to obtain a slurry, and separating the slurry into a solid component and a liquid component; (4) removing water from the solid component by subjecting the solid component to heating and/or a reduced-pressure environment, wherein an ambient pressure at which the solid component exists and/or a temperature thereof is changed so that a ratio of saturated water vapor pressure at the temperature thereof to the ambient pressure becomes 80% or more within 24 hours after slurry separation; and (5) heat-treating the solid component after step (4) at lower than 900° C.

US20260011742A1.title: IRON-BASED CATHODE LITHIUM-SUPPLEMENTING MATERIAL, PREPARATION THEREOF, AND APPLICATION THEREOF
US20260011742A1.abstract: An iron-based cathode lithium-supplementing material, a preparation method thereof, and an application thereof. The iron-based cathode lithium-supplementing material includes a lithium-rich iron-based core and a passivation layer coated on a surface of the lithium-rich iron-based core. A material of the passivation layer includes LiFeO2 and LidMOe, d and e satisfy d≥2e−5, and the M is selected from at least one element of Al, Ni, Mn, Co, Ti, Zr, Si, V, Zn, Cr, Cu, and P. LiFeO2 and LidMOe form a passivation layer on the surface of the lithium-rich iron-based core, and the passivation layer has high density and does not react with water, and can have effect in isolating harmful components, such as water and carbon dioxide, in the air, ensuring the effect of isolating the material from water vapor during storage, and has stable performance, which is conducive to widespread application.

US20260016230A1.title: METHOD FOR SETTING AN OVEN ATMOSPHERE IN A HEAT-TREATMENT OVEN
US20260016230A1.abstract: A method of establishing a furnace atmosphere in a directly heated heat treatment furnace is provided. A heat treatment furnace has at least one burner which is operated with a fuel gas and an oxygenous gas that are combusted to give a combustion gas. The combustion gas has a defined composition having a defined partial water vapor pressure. Hydrogen is used in the fuel gas with a proportion of at least 10% by volume. The heat treatment furnace is additionally flooded with a water vapor-free and/or hydrogen-free gas, as a result of which the water vapor-free and/or hydrogen-free gas mixes with the combustion gas so as to bring about a partial water vapor pressure of the mixture in the furnace atmosphere of the heat treatment furnace that is less than the defined partial water vapor pressure of the combustion gas.

PL449167A1.title: Volatile organic compounds and water vapor sensor and method of manufacturing a sensor electrode matrix from Mₙ₊₁XₙTₓ type materials
PL449167A1.abstract: Przedmiotem zgłoszenia jest sensor lotnych związków organicznych oraz pary wodnej, a także sposób wytwarzania elektrod sensora z materiałów typu Mn+1XnTx. Sensor ten umożliwia detekcję, identyfikację i określenie stężeń lotnych związków organicznych w atmosferze powietrza, jak również gazów obojętnych przy różnym poziomie wilgotności względnej. Sensor posiada komorę sensorową połączoną z blokiem sterująco-odczytowym i z blokiem analizy. W komorze znajduje się element grzejny, na którym umieszczona jest matryca składającą się od 2 do 20 elektrod, posiadających podłoże z ceramiki Al2O3, na którym znajduje się warstwa materiału typu MXene, o grubości co najmniej 100 nm, domieszkowana jonami pierwiastków z grupy 4 - 12 układu okresowego. Sposób wykonania matrycy elektrod sensora polega na tym, że najpierw przygotowuje się za pomocą syntezy chemicznej materiał Mn+1XnTx, gdzie x wynosi 1, 2 albo 3. Następnie materiał ten nanosi się na 2 do 20 podłoży z ceramiki Al2O3 i suszy się. Później warstwę Mn+1XnTx znajdującą się na podłożach poddaje się implantacji jonami pierwiastków z grup 4 - 12 układu okresowego. Na zakończenie, na poszczególnych elektrodach matrycy wykonuje się kontakty elektryczne.

UA161811U.title: CUTTING METHOD IN DIRECT POLARITY OF METAL SHEETS
UA161811U.abstract: Plasma cutting method in direct polarity of metal sheets with water usage involves supplying water to the hollow electrode of the plasma torch through capillary holes, with a water flow rate of 0.035...0.055 l/min, and the specific value of the water flow rate is determined depending on the thickness of the metal plate being cut and the capillary holes. The water evaporates under the action of heat, first from the auxiliary arc and then from the cutting arc, after which it mixes with the vortex of plasma-forming gas to form a gas mixture containing 20% water vapor. The auxiliary arc is ignited between the hollow electrode of the plasma torch and the electrode-nozzle, which are separated by an electrically insulated cyclone with tangential holes for supplying plasma-forming gas. The cutting arc is ignited between the hollow electrode of the plasma torch and the metal being cut. In this case, water is supplied along the axis of a hollow copper cathode electrode, which has a ring-shaped hafnium insert at its working end, with the water evaporation zone located outside the cathode spot zone inside the cavity of the copper cathode electrode, and its depth relative to the hafnium insert does not exceed 1-2 mm.

GB2642940A.title: Chemical coolant for aerosol free extinguishing agent and preparation method
GB2642940A.abstract: Disclosed are a chemical coolant for an aerosol fire extinguishing agent and a preparation method. The chemical coolant is prepared by means of selecting an inorganic salt of K/Na/Mg/Ca/Sr that can undergo a decomposition reaction and combining same with an organic flame retardant and a binder. By using a metal inorganic salt, a decomposition reaction occurs at a high temperature to generate active metal substances such as K/Na/Mg/Ca/Sr oxide, and the fire extinguishing capacity is enhanced while combustion heat is absorbed. The organic flame retardant also undergoes a vaporization or decomposition reaction at a high temperature to generate a non-combustible gas such as NH3, N2, CO2, and water vapor, the aerosol content is increased while flame extinction and cooling are achieved, and the fire extinguishing capacity is enhanced. Furthermore, the organic flame retardant also has certain binding characteristics. An auxiliary binder is used for binding the chemical coolant to increase the stability, thereby preventing the metal inorganic salt in the chemical coolant from experiencing pulverization during storage, transportation, and use.

US12508547B1.title: System for desalinating water with multiple desalination processes
US12508547B1.abstract: A system for water desalination includes a membrane module and an ejector module. The membrane module includes a feed tank for salt water, a heater, a humidifier receiving heated salt water and a carrier gas to form a wet gas, and a membrane distillation unit. The membrane distillation unit includes a feed chamber, a membrane, and a permeate chamber with a sweeping gas inlet. The membrane allows water vapor to pass from the feed chamber to the permeate chamber. The ejector module includes an ejector and a bubble column dehumidifier. The ejector has first and second inlets for primary and secondary fluid streams, respectively, creating a mixed stream. The bubble column dehumidifier receives the mixed stream to produce fresh water. The second inlet of the ejector receives both wet gas from the humidifier and sweeping gas from the permeate chamber as the secondary fluid stream.

WO2026007818A1.title: DISPOSITIF DE BAIN DE PIED
WO2026007818A1.abstract: The present invention relates to a foot bath device. The massage assemblies of existing foot bath devices feature a poor user experience. The present invention comprises a housing provided with a foot bath cavity therein. A sauna assembly is disposed in the foot bath cavity. High-temperature water vapor generated by the sauna assembly diffuses in the foot bath cavity and surrounds the feet of a user. A massage assembly is disposed in the housing and extracts, pressurizes, and sprays the high-temperature water vapor to form fluid beams, and the fluid beams flush the feet to generate massage stimulation. The massage assembly is configured for forming massage stimulation by spraying the fluid beams. The massage assembly pressurizes and sprays the high-temperature water vapor to form fluid beams having kinetic energy and thermal energy, so that multiple massage stimulations, including pressing stimulation and thermal stimulation, can be applied to the feet by using the fluid beams, thereby improving massage comfort. In addition, the high-temperature water vapor in the foot bath cavity can also be cyclically heated for use, reducing water consumption and maintaining the temperature in the foot bath cavity and thereby improving the user experience.

WO2026006823A1.title: HYDROGELS AUTO-IRRIGUANTS À LIBÉRATION LENTE D'ENGRAIS POUR L'AGRICULTURE DURABLE
WO2026006823A1.abstract: Given the critical need for more efficient water and nutrient utilization to optimize crop yields, effective strategies in water and nutrient management are essential. The development of a self-irrigation and slow-release fertilizer hydrogel (SISRH) represents a promising approach. SISRH, a hydrogel with hygroscopic polymer chains interpenetrated by a thermo-responsive network, enhances plant growth through controlled yet self-sustained water and nutrient delivery. The hydrogel demonstrates diurnal functionality: it absorbs water vapor at night and releases it during the day due to a phase transition in the polymer, with incorporated calcium chloride (CaCl2) further improving hygroscopic properties and controlled nutrient release. SISRH facilitates a slow-release of nutrients and can achieve water savings, significantly reducing the need for frequent irrigation and ensuring robust crop development.

WO2026007011A1.title: SYSTÈME ET PROCÉDÉ DE MODIFICATION DE CARBONATATION EN SUSPENSION À BASSE PRESSION DE MATÉRIAU PULVÉRULENT COMBINÉ À UNE RÉDUCTION D'ÉMISSION DE GAZ DE COMBUSTION INDUSTRIEL CO2
WO2026007011A1.abstract: Disclosed in the present invention are a system and method for low-pressure suspension carbonation modification of a powdery material combined with emission reduction of industrial flue gas CO2, comprising a low-pressure suspension carbonation bed system, a bag dust collector, and an induced draft fan. The low-pressure suspension carbonation bed system comprises an air duct, a suspension carbonation bed, and a water spraying system. High-temperature and high-CO2 flue gas and powdery material are sprayed upward into the suspension carbonation bed, and water or an aqueous solution of a carbonation aid is sprayed into the suspension carbonation bed in the form of droplets, so that the water vapor in the flue gas forms a critical saturation state, forming a liquid film on the surface of the material. The CO2 in the flue gas dissolves into the liquid film to undergo a carbonation reaction with the material. The flue gas carries the material and enters the bag dust collector, where part of the collected powder returns to the low-pressure suspension carbonation bed system for cyclic carbonation, and part is used as a modified carbonated material. The present invention achieves sufficient carbonation of powdery material and flue gas in a suspended state, improves the carbonation rate, reduces the emission of CO2 in the flue gas, and is conducive to large-scale and continuous production of material carbonation processes.

WO2026000592A1.abstract: The present application relates to an electrode-sheet drying device and a battery production apparatus. The electrode-sheet drying device comprises: a heating assembly, which comprises a heat-insulating cover and a plurality of heating units arranged at intervals in the heat-insulating cover, all the heating units being used for heating an electrode sheet, and the heating assembly further comprising air extraction pipes, which are in communication with a cavity formed by the heat-insulating cover so as to extract water vapor from the cavity; and a conveying mechanism, which comprises a conveying member for movably passing through the heat-insulating cover, wherein the conveying member is used for carrying and conveying the electrode sheet, so as to drive the electrode sheet to enter or leave the heat-insulating cover, a vacuum cavity is formed inside the conveying member, and suction holes in communication with the vacuum cavity are provided in the surface of the conveying member. US2025388822A1.title: METHODS FOR PRODUCING MONOCYCLIC AROMATIC HYDROCARBON, TEREPHTHALIC ACID, AND POLYETHYLENE TEREPHTHALATE, AND MANAGEMENT METHODS THEREFOR
US2025388822A1.abstract: The invention provides a method capable of producing a monocyclic aromatic hydrocarbon such as benzene or xylene effective as a base raw material, with a naphtha raw material containing renewable naphtha. The method for producing at least one monocyclic aromatic hydrocarbon of benzene or xylene with a naphtha raw material containing renewable naphtha includes step (A-1) of pyrolyzing the naphtha raw material in the presence of water vapor, to produce and separate toluene, and step (A-2) of subjecting toluene to disproportionation reaction or transalkylation reaction, to produce and separate at least one monocyclic aromatic hydrocarbon of benzene or xylene.

CN121182042A.title: Polyolefin resin composition as well as preparation method and application thereof
CN121182042A.abstract: The invention discloses a polyolefin resin composition as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The polyolefin resin composition comprises the following components in parts by weight: 30-70 parts of polyolefin resin; 3 to 10 parts of a polyolefin maleic anhydride graft; 60 to 120 parts of a halogen-free flame retardant; 4 to 15 parts of zinc hydroxystannate; and 4-15 parts of hollow glass beads. The zinc hydroxystannate in the polyolefin resin composition has more hydroxyl groups, and can be decomposed to generate water vapor to take away a large amount of heat when being heated, so that the flame retardant property of the material can be improved; meanwhile, after being matched with the hollow glass beads, the flame retardant can achieve a synergistic flame-retardant effect with resin and a flame retardant, so that the anti-dripping capability is improved. The hollow glass beads are easier to soften at a high temperature and can form carbon at a lower temperature, so that the flame-retardant effect is achieved in a shorter time, the heat release peak value is effectively reduced, the emergency window period is prolonged, and salt mist and toxic gas are reduced.

CN121191989A.title: Bump packaging structure and packaging method
CN121191989A.abstract: The invention discloses a bump packaging structure and a packaging method. The packaging method comprises the following steps: step 1, sputtering a seed layer on the surface of a wafer; 2, manufacturing a photoresist opening in the surface of the wafer; 3, at least two metal layers are manufactured in the photoresist opening, so that the Pad is connected out; 4, removing the seed layer on the surface of the wafer to enable the salient points to be directly independent, and avoiding short circuit; and step 5, completing coating, grinding and cutting of the metal layer structure on the surface of the salient point, and manufacturing the whole wafer into a single packaging chip. According to the invention, a compact nickel-gold layer can be formed on the surface of the salient point, corrosion of water vapor to copper is prevented, galvanic effect in a chip cutting process is avoided, the reliability passing rate of the chip in a high-humidity environment is improved, and the preparation method is simple, convenient and suitable for industrial popularization and application.

CN121183565A.title: Green and efficient activation method of graphite felt electrode for all-vanadium redox flow battery
CN121183565A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of flow batteries, and particularly relates to a green and efficient activation method of a graphite felt electrode for an all-vanadium flow battery. Firstly, a graphite felt electrode is placed in an ozone environment to be heated for pre-activation treatment, so that the surface of the graphite felt electrode is provided with oxygen-containing groups, the surface affinity is improved, and the subsequent activation temperature is reduced; a graphite felt pre-activated by ozone is placed in a tubular furnace, a certain amount of water vapor is introduced when the temperature is raised to a certain temperature in a nitrogen atmosphere, so that graphite felt fibers are etched and activated at a high temperature, oxygen-containing functional groups are anchored, defects are increased, and the specific surface area and reaction activity of the electrode are improved. And when the material is applied to the all-vanadium redox flow battery, the energy efficiency of the battery can be effectively improved. The method is simple, green in process, high in practicability and suitable for large-scale production.

CN223550254U.title: Ultralow-temperature liquid hydrogen storage cabin
CN223550254U.abstract: An ultralow-temperature liquid hydrogen storage cabin is provided with a cylindrical inner cabin, the inner cabin is supported and fixed in an outer cabin through a filling layer, the inner cabin and the outer cabin form a filling layer space, and the filling layer space is filled with low-temperature LNG or low-temperature liquid nitrogen. The inner cabin is composed of an inner cabin outer wall and an inner cabin inner wall, the inner cabin outer wall and the inner cabin inner wall form a vacuum layer, and the vacuum layer is filled with perlite and vacuumized. A double-layer compartment is arranged, an insulating layer is laid on an outer compartment, an inner compartment is of a vacuum double-wall heat insulation structure, and the space between the inner compartment and the outer compartment is filled with LNG or liquid nitrogen with the temperature close to that of liquid hydrogen. According to the scheme, the ultralow-temperature liquid state maintenance of-163 DEG C LNG or-196 DEG C liquid ammonia medium is easily realized through a mature reliquefaction system, so that the liquid state maintenance of the ultralow-temperature-253 DEG C liquid hydrogen medium is indirectly ensured, the heat transfer between the liquid hydrogen cabin and the external environment can be greatly reduced, and the technical difficulty and the technical cost of ultralow-temperature storage of the liquid hydrogen are reduced.

CN223498717U.title: Emergency cut-off stop valve for liquid hydrogen
CN223498717U.abstract: The utility model provides an emergency cut-off stop valve for liquid hydrogen, which comprises a valve body, a valve clack, a fixer and a sealing ring, and the valve clack is arranged in the valve body; the valve clack is suitable for abutting against the side wall of a runner opening in the valve body. The fixer is connected with the bottom of the valve clack; the sealing ring is arranged between the valve clack and the fixator and is configured to protrude out of the circumferential side of the valve clack, and the part, protruding out of the circumferential side of the valve clack, of the sealing ring is suitable for abutting against the side wall of a runner opening in the valve body and sealing the runner opening; the side wall depth of a flow channel opening in the valve body is larger than the width of the sealing ring protruding out of the circumferential side of the valve clack. The depth of the side wall of the flow channel opening in the valve body is larger than the width of the part, protruding out of the circumferential side of the valve clack, of the sealing ring, after the sealing ring loses efficacy due to excessive abrasion, effective sealing can still be formed through tight attaching between the valve clack and the side wall of the flow channel opening in the valve body, and liquid hydrogen medium leakage is prevented.

CN223178624U.title: Normal-pressure storage tank for storing liquid hydrogen
CN223178624U.abstract: The utility model provides a normal-pressure storage tank for storing liquid hydrogen. The normal-pressure storage tank comprises an inner tank made of metal and used for containing a low-temperature liquid hydrogen medium, an outer tank located outside the inner tank and made of metal, a heat insulation interlayer located in an interlayer between the inner tank and the outer tank and playing a heat insulation role, and a cold shield arranged in the heat insulation interlayer. The cold shield comprises a cold shield coil pipe which is made of a metal material and is coiled in the heat insulation interlayer, the inlet end of the cold shield coil pipe is connected with a boil-off gas outlet of the gas phase space at the top of the inner tank, and the outlet end of the cold shield coil pipe penetrates through the outer tank, extends out of the outer tank and is connected with a boil-off gas recovery device. The cold shield medium entering the cold shield is liquid hydrogen evaporated gas in the inner tank, the defect of high daily evaporation rate of a liquid hydrogen low-temperature normal-pressure storage technology can be overcome without introducing an external cold source, and the operation cost of the normal-pressure storage tank is reduced.

CN120717458A.title: Graphene preparation system and method
CN120717458A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of graphene preparation, and provides a graphene preparation system and method.The system comprises a gas supply device, a plasma synthesis device, a gas-solid separation device, a product collection device, a tail gas separation device and a hydrogen storage device. The invention relates to a graphene preparation method, which comprises the following steps: ionizing a medium gas to generate plasma, and introducing a reaction gas to carry out a synthesis reaction; separating the reacted gas from a reaction product; settling and collecting the obtained reaction product; separating the obtained gas into hydrogen, medium gas and reaction gas; the medium gas and the reaction gas are recycled after being compressed, and the separated hydrogen is purified and stored. According to the technical scheme, the production cost is reduced, the material handling capacity is large, the productivity is high, the method is suitable for industrial application, energy consumption is low, pollution is avoided, expandability is high, and the application range is wide.
