CN121185611A.title: Check valve state monitoring management system and method for high-pressure hydrogen environment
CN121185611A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of valves, and discloses a check valve state monitoring management system and method for a high-pressure hydrogen environment. The method is used for monitoring and managing the state of the check valve and solves the problem of insufficient reliability of the current test result of the in-service check valve. Comprising a valve group module, a check valve information module, a check valve monitoring history module, a check valve maintenance history module, a performance analysis and evaluation module and a state monitoring planning and implementation module. And the performance analysis and evaluation module is used for analyzing test and maintenance data in the check valve monitoring history module and the maintenance history module, and setting a reference for in-service test, inspection and preventive maintenance of the valve group. And the state monitoring plan and implementation module carries out test, inspection and preventive maintenance of the in-service check valve according to the state monitoring plan, and evaluates the performance and state of the in-service check valve. The reliability of the in-service check valve is improved, and the in-service check valve has wide application prospects.

CN121184352A.title: Diaphragm testing device and method for hydrogen diaphragm type compressor
CN121184352A.abstract: The invention provides a diaphragm testing device and method for a hydrogen diaphragm type compressor, and the device comprises a hydrogen source, a fatigue stretching assembly and a recovery tank, hydrogen in the hydrogen source enters the fatigue stretching assembly after being pressurized, and the fatigue stretching assembly is used for driving a testing diaphragm to move up and down in a hydrogen environment. The recovery tank is used for recovering hydrogen flowing out of the fatigue stretching assembly, the fatigue stretching assembly comprises a stretching rod, a piston connected to the bottom end of the stretching rod and arranged in the test membrane cavity and an electric cylinder connected to the top end of the stretching rod, and the electric cylinder is used for driving the stretching rod to drive the piston to move up and down in the test membrane cavity in the vertical direction; and a dynamic load is applied to the diaphragm in the test diaphragm cavity. The vibration frequency and amplitude of the diaphragm can be accurately controlled through the fatigue stretching assembly.

CN121216331A.title: Pouring bus unit and bus duct system for hydrogen energy environment
CN121216331A.abstract: The invention provides a pouring bus unit and a bus duct system used for a hydrogen energy environment, and relates to the technical field of power transmission equipment, the pouring bus unit used for the hydrogen energy environment comprises at least one busbar conductor and an insulation pouring bus duct integrally formed through a pouring technology, and the number of the busbar conductor is at least one; the busbar conductor is fixed on the inner side of the insulating pouring bus duct, and the insulating pouring bus duct is made of a polymer-based composite insulating material; the bus duct system comprises the pouring bus unit used for the hydrogen energy environment. The insulation pouring bus duct is formed by pouring the polymer-based composite insulation material, on one hand, the stability of the installation structure of the busbar conductor can be effectively guaranteed, the protection effect is improved, on the other hand, the insulation reliability and the anti-hydrogen environment adaptability are excellent, the use process is safe and reliable, meanwhile, the manufacturing process is simple, and the production cost is low. CN121093692A.title: Crystal plasticity and cohesion model coupling hydrogen induced cracking simulation method based on test
CN121093692A.abstract: The invention discloses a test-based crystal plasticity and cohesion model coupling hydrogen induced cracking simulation method, which comprises the following steps: carrying out a hydrogen filling mechanical property test on a metal material to obtain a true stress-true strain curve of the metal material in a hydrogen environment; carrying out a hydrogen-filled fracture toughness test on the metal material to obtain a stress intensity factor of the metal material in a hydrogen environment; constructing a cohesion constitutive model; a metal material electron back scattering diffraction microscopic characterization test is carried out, microstructure characteristics are obtained, and a polycrystalline model is constructed; constructing a crystal plastic constitutive model; coupling crystal plasticity constitutive and cohesion constitutive, and calibrating crystal plasticity to-be-determined parameters by adopting a trial-and-error method; and carrying out finite element simulation analysis on hydrogen-induced cracking behaviors with different microstructure characteristics in a hydrogen environment to obtain stress-strain data and crack data of the hydrogen-induced cracking behaviors. According to the invention, high-precision simulation of the hydrogen-induced cracking micromechanism of the metal material in the hydrogen environment is realized.

CN121160185A.title: Hydrogen permeation resistant composite coating as well as preparation method and application thereof
CN121160185A.abstract: The invention relates to the field of composite materials, and discloses an anti-hydrogen permeation composite coating as well as a preparation method and application thereof. The hydrogen permeation resistant composite coating is prepared from the following raw materials: epoxy resin, carboxyl-terminated liquid nitrile rubber, polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane and an ionic liquid modified layered nano composite material. Wherein the layered nano composite material is graphite phase carbon nitride and/or cobalt-aluminum double metal hydroxide; and the thickness of the layered nano composite material is 2 to 100 nm. According to the present invention, the coating material with characteristics of excellent internal resistance reducing effect and hydrogen permeation resistance is prepared mainly through the design of the polymer matrix material and the compounding of the functional filler, and can be used in all devices and pipelines in the hydrogen environment, such that the intrinsic safety performance is improved, and the service life is prolonged.

CN121226171A.title: Synthesis method of 4-(4-methylphenoxy) benzylamine salt
CN121226171A.abstract: The invention provides a synthetic method of 4-(4-methylphenoxy) benzylamine salt, and belongs to the field of organic synthesis. The invention provides a synthesis method of 4-(4-methylphenoxy) benzylamine hydrochloride, which comprises the following steps: mixing 4-(4-methylphenoxy) benzonitrile, inorganic alkali, supported nickel and an alcohol solvent, then carrying out addition reaction in a hydrogen environment, filtering the obtained addition reaction system, then adjusting the acid of the obtained filtrate, and then crystallizing to obtain the 4-(4-methylphenoxy) benzylamine hydrochloride. And the 4-(4-methyl phenoxy) benzylamine hydrochloride is obtained. Compared with the prior art, the synthesis method provided by the invention has the advantages that the used raw materials are sufficient in supply and low in price, and both the yield and the purity are higher than those of general levels: the 4-(4-methylphenoxy) benzylamine hydrochloride is obtained by taking the 4-(4-methylphenoxy) benzonitrile as the raw material, the supported nickel as the catalyst and the inorganic base as the alkaline auxiliary agent and adjusting the acid and crystallizing by using hydrochloric acid, the operation is simple, the cost is low, and the method is suitable for industrial production. The raw materials are small in pollution and low in toxicity, the yield is stabilized at 97%, and the purity is 99% or above.

CN121007767A.title: Method and system for synergistically testing and evaluating fatigue and fracture of metal material in hydrogen environment
CN121007767A.abstract: The invention relates to a hydrogen environment metal material fatigue and fracture cooperative test and evaluation method and system, and belongs to the field of metal material hydrogen environment compatibility. The method comprises the following steps: performing a fatigue crack propagation test and a fracture toughness test on a fatigue and fracture synergistic test sample in a hydrogen environment with preset pressure and preset temperature; based on the obtained fatigue crack propagation curve and the obtained crack propagation resistance curve, obtaining a fatigue crack propagation fitting curve and a fracture toughness characteristic value in the hydrogen environment with the preset pressure and the preset temperature; and based on the fatigue crack propagation fitting curve and the fracture toughness characteristic value in the hydrogen environment with the preset pressure and the preset temperature, safety evaluation of the metal material simulated hydrogen conveying pipeline under the fatigue load working condition is completed. The fatigue crack test is replaced by the fracture toughness test according to the defect length, the fatigue and fracture cooperative test is realized, the sample replacement is not needed, the test material is saved, the test preparation time for the sample replacement and cooling process is reduced, and the test efficiency is improved.

CN223707885U.title: Compressor hydrogen environment testing device
CN223707885U.abstract: The utility model provides a compressor hydrogen environment testing device, which particularly relates to the technical field of compressor testing and comprises a hydrogen supply module, a compressor testing module, a safety control module and a recycling module. The hydrogen supply module is responsible for conveying hydrogen to the compressor test module; the compressor test module is connected with the hydrogen supply module and used for compressing hydrogen to test compressor performance; the safety control module is connected with the compressor test module, regulates and controls the air pressure in the hydrogen conveying process and extinguishes sparks in the hydrogen conveying process; the recycling module is connected with the compressor testing module and the safety control module, and recycling of hydrogen is achieved. The device solves the safety problem that the performance of the compressor cannot be tested in the hydrogen environment, the test safety is enhanced, the test period is shortened, and the cost is reduced.

CN120992355A.abstract: The invention provides a rock salt creep experiment device in a high-pressure hydrogen environment, and belongs to the technical field of rock experiments, the rock salt creep experiment device comprises a cavity, the top and the side of the cavity are provided with openings, a top cover and a side cover, the top cover and the side cover are used for sealing the two openings respectively, and the side cover is provided with an observation window; an air injection hole and an exhaust hole are formed in one side, deviating from the side cover, of the side wall of the cavity, and the air injection hole is higher than the exhaust hole; the experiment assembling structure is arranged in the cavity, and the experiment assembling structure comprises a sample placing table, a limiting assembly and a loading rod; the gas injection and recovery system is arranged on one side of the cavity and used for injecting high-pressure hydrogen into the cavity and recovering the high-pressure hydrogen in the cavity; and the shooting system is arranged on one side where the side cover of the cavity is located and comprises a shooting device and a light source. CN121137509A.title: High-temperature hydrogen GETTER device based on surface passivation barrier layer
CN121137509A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of gas purification, and particularly relates to a high-temperature hydrogen GETTER device based on a surface passivation barrier layer. A compact Cr2O3 passivation barrier layer formed through high-temperature oxidation pretreatment is arranged on the surface of a pressure-bearing metal component in the device, and the thickness of the compact Cr2O3 passivation barrier layer is 0.5-5 microns. The pretreatment process comprises the following steps of: performing surface finishing to ensure that the roughness Ra is less than or equal to 0.4 mu m, heating to 850-1050 DEG C at the speed of 5-10 DEG C/min in an inert atmosphere with the vacuum degree of less than or equal to 10 <-3 > mbar or the dew point of less than or equal to-40 DEG C, preserving heat for 1-4 hours, and cooling to 200 DEG C or below at the speed of less than or equal to 5 DEG C/min. The device further comprises a multi-stage purging system, a uniform flow channel design is adopted, and ultra-pure hydrogen with the methane content smaller than or equal to 0.1 ppb is used as purging gas. The hydrogen permeation path is fundamentally blocked, the methane content of an outlet can be stably controlled below 1ppb in the hydrogen environment of 600 DEG C and 10 bar, the hydrogen corrosion resistance of equipment is remarkably improved, the service life of the equipment is remarkably prolonged, and the hydrogen permeation device is suitable for the fields of semiconductor manufacturing, new energy and the like with extremely high hydrogen purity requirements.

CN121068327A.title: Method for detecting compatibility of metal welded joint and hydrogen environment
CN121068327A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of safety evaluation of natural gas hydrogen conveying pipelines, and particularly discloses a method for detecting compatibility of a metal welded joint and a hydrogen environment, which comprises the following steps: placing a tensile sample in a reaction device, and injecting test gas into the reaction device to simulate and establish a test environment; the test environment is perfected based on the environment parameters obtained in the test environment; under the condition that the tensile sample is placed in a test environment or a control environment, carrying out slow strain rate stretching on the tensile sample by adopting a constant displacement rate loading mode; acquiring test environment tensile data of the tensile sample based on the test environment; or acquiring contrast environment tensile data of the tensile sample based on the contrast environment; and performing hydrogen environment compatibility analysis based on the test environment tensile data and the control environment tensile data.

CN120844024A.title: High-pressure piston type hydrogen compressor cylinder surface PVD coating and preparation process thereof
CN120844024A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of compressor cylinder body surface coating, and discloses a high-pressure piston type hydrogen compressor cylinder body surface PVD preparation process which comprises the following steps: step 1, matrix laser microtexturing and plasma activation: machining micro-pit arrays on the surfaces of a cylinder sleeve and a piston by adopting a pulse fiber laser; and the oxide layer is removed through Ar < + >/H2 mixed plasma etching, and a nanoscale activated surface is generated. According to the method, the film-substrate binding force is remarkably enhanced through laser microtexture and plasma activation, and the coating is prevented from being peeled off; by utilizing a multi-layer composite structure and a rare earth doping technology, the coating structure is extremely densified, a hydrogen permeation path is effectively blocked, and hydrogen embrittlement is fundamentally inhibited; gradient design and fluorinated hydrophobic treatment are adopted, so that the surface is endowed with low friction coefficient and super-hydrophobicity, and abrasion and corrosion are reduced; meanwhile, high hardness, high toughness and excellent corrosion resistance are considered, and the reliability, durability and safety of the cylinder body in the high-pressure hydrogen environment are comprehensively improved.

CN120777373A.title: Hydrogen embrittlement-resistant double-piston-effect high-pressure ball valve and intelligent pressure relief method thereof
CN120777373A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of sealing valves, in particular to a hydrogen embrittlement-resistant double-piston-effect high-pressure ball valve and an intelligent pressure relief method thereof, and can solve the problems that a traditional high-pressure ball valve is poor in sealing performance, difficult to adapt to pressure change, incapable of meeting the intelligent characteristic, difficult to achieve accurate pressure control and poor in pressure relief effect to a certain extent. The hydrogen embrittlement failure is easily caused. The ball valve comprises a double-piston-effect valve seat, a front valve seat and a rear valve seat of the double-piston-effect valve seat float independently to form double piston rings, and a discharge channel with the diameter of 2.0 +/-0.1 mm is integrated; the triple dynamic sealing system covers metal-to-metal primary sealing, PTFE and expanded graphite composite ring secondary sealing and hastelloy corrugated pipe fire sealing; and the valve body is subjected to subzero treatment. According to the intelligent pressure relief method, the pressure gradient is monitored in real time through a piezoelectric sensor; and when the pressure is 2.0 MPa, the piezoelectric actuator expands the hole diameter of the discharge hole. According to the invention, the overpressure explosion risk of a valve cavity is solved, a hydrogen permeation path is blocked, hydrogen embrittlement is eliminated, and 15,000 times of zero-leakage circulation in a 70MPa hydrogen environment is realized.

CN120925044A.title: High-performance material combination for ultrahigh-speed permanent magnet motor driven hydrogen circulating pump and application process
CN120925044A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of ultrahigh-speed motor materials and hydrogen energy equipment, in particular to a high-performance material combination for an ultrahigh-speed permanent magnet motor driven hydrogen circulating pump, which comprises a permanent magnet material: a neodymium iron boron 42SH magnet is adopted and subjected to Dy-Cu co-doping treatment, and the surface of the magnet is coated with a nickel-phosphorus alloy coating of 10-15 microns; the rotor sheath is made of TC11 titanium alloy (containing 3.5%-4.5% of Mo), and the hardness of the TC11 titanium alloy reaches 38-42 HRC after beta heat treatment; a pump body and an impeller are made of a combined structure of Hastelloy C276 and a SiC particle reinforced aluminum matrix composite (the volume fraction of SiC is 15%-20%), and the inner wall of the pump body is sprayed with a 50-80-micron diamond-like carbon (DLC) coating; hydrogen embrittlement resistance and stability are enhanced, the hydrogen embrittlement critical value of a key material is increased to 200 MPa.m < 1/2 > or above, and the continuous operation life under the 1.5 MPa hydrogen environment is longer than or equal to 8000 h and is prolonged by two times compared with a traditional material; the high-temperature magnetic performance attenuation rate of the permanent magnet is smaller than or equal to 5%, the fatigue strength of the rotor sheath is improved by 25%, and the requirement for long-term operation at the speed of 200000r/min is met.

CN120736996A.title: Method for preparing p-aminobenzoic acid through chlorination, hydrolysis and hydrogenation of p-nitrotoluene
CN120736996A.abstract: The invention provides a method for preparing p-aminobenzoic acid through chlorination, hydrolysis and hydrogenation of p-nitrotoluene, and belongs to the technical field of organic compound preparation. Comprising the following steps: melting p-nitrotoluene, and performing chlorination reaction under the catalysis of azodiisobutyronitrile to generate a chlorinated product containing p-nitrobenzyl chloride; mixing the chlorination product with liquid caustic soda and sodium hypochlorite for hydrolysis reaction, separating liquid after reaction, and acidifying an upper-layer water phase to obtain p-nitrobenzoic acid; the method comprises the following steps: mixing p-nitrobenzoic acid with methanol and a Pd/C catalyst, carrying out hydrogenation reaction in a hydrogen environment, and filtering, distilling, cooling, centrifuging and drying after the reaction to obtain p-aminobenzoic acid. The method is simple in process route, mild in reaction condition, high in product purity, high in yield and suitable for industrial production, raw materials are easy to obtain, hydrogen chloride gas generated in the chlorination reaction and p-nitrotoluene obtained after the hydrolysis reaction are recycled, cyclic utilization of resources is achieved, and the production cost is reduced.

CN120741142A.title: Hydrogen environment metal material constant load testing device and CTOD-based testing method
CN120741142A.abstract: The invention discloses a hydrogen environment metal material constant load testing device and a CTOD-based testing method.The high-pressure gas storage bin in the device is connected with an upper bin cover and a lower bin cover in a sealed mode, the upper bin cover is provided with a gas inlet pipeline, the lower bin cover is provided with a gas outlet pipeline, the high-pressure gas storage bin is filled with hydrogen, the upper bin cover is provided with an upper loading rod, and the upper loading rod is provided with a lower loading rod; the upper end of the upper loading rod is connected with a mechanical testing machine loading head, the lower end of the loading rod extends into the high-pressure gas storage bin, a load sensor is installed on the upper loading rod in the high-pressure gas storage bin, a lower loading rod is arranged on the lower bin cover, the upper end of the lower loading rod extends into the high-pressure gas storage bin, and the lower end of the lower loading rod is connected with a mechanical testing machine base. A constant-load sample is connected between the upper loading rod and the lower loading rod, an extensometer is mounted on the constant-load sample, and a temperature sensor and a pressure sensor are mounted in the high-pressure gas storage bin. The device can effectively provide a high-pressure hydrogen test environment, the method is not limited by the wall thickness of the pipe, and the fracture toughness value of the sample in the hydrogen environment is obtained.

CN120741283A.abstract: The invention discloses a full-size pressure regulator hydrogen permeation and pressure regulating performance testing integrated device which comprises hydrogen/hydrogen-doped natural gas, a gas source, a valve set, a tee joint, a pressure gauge set, a pipeline, a sealing clamp, a pressure regulator, a gas chromatograph and a vacuum pump. CN120934218A.title: Hydrogen circulating pump stator core structure adopting 0.05 mm silicon steel sheets
CN120934218A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of motor iron core structures, in particular to a hydrogen circulating pump stator iron core structure adopting 0.05 mm silicon steel sheets. The hydrogen circulating pump stator iron core structure adopting 0.05 mm silicon steel sheets comprises an iron core body which is formed by laminating a plurality of silicon steel sheets with the thickness of 0.05 mm in the axial direction, and the surfaces of the silicon steel sheets are provided with insulating coatings; the stator yoke part is located on the periphery of the iron core body and is of an annular structure, and the thickness of the yoke part is 15-20 mm; the stator tooth parts are uniformly distributed along the inner circumference of the stator yoke part, the number of the stator tooth parts is 12-24, the width of each tooth part is 5-8mm, and a wire slot is formed between every two adjacent tooth parts; the eddy current path is shortened by the ultra-thin silicon steel sheets of 0.05 mm, and the high-frequency iron loss is reduced by more than 40% and the motor efficiency is improved by 5-8% by combining high-frequency low-loss materials; and the heat dissipation grooves and the graphene heat-conducting fins have a synergistic effect, so that the temperature rise of the iron core is reduced by 15-20 DEG C, and high-temperature potential safety hazards in a hydrogen environment are avoided.

CN120741215A.title: Method for testing hydrogen induced cracking of material in high-pressure hydrogen environment
CN120741215A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of material testing, and discloses a method for testing hydrogen induced cracking of a material in a high-pressure hydrogen environment, which comprises the following steps: firstly, preparing an extended compact tensile sample based on size data of a standard wedge-shaped opening loading sample and a standard compact tensile sample; then carrying out fatigue crack prefabrication on the extended compact tensile sample through a fatigue testing machine, and carrying out shape conversion to obtain a standard wedge-shaped opening loading sample with fatigue cracks, and taking the standard wedge-shaped opening loading sample as a target wedge-shaped opening loading sample; applying a load to the tail end of the fatigue crack on the target wedge-shaped opening loading sample, and placing the target wedge-shaped opening loading sample in a high-pressure hydrogen environment to determine the length of the current hydrogen-induced crack; and finally, determining a stress intensity factor threshold value of the to-be-tested material according to the initial applied stress intensity factor, the current hydrogen-induced crack length and the standard value of the hydrogen-induced crack length. According to the method, not only can a more accurate hydrogen-induced delayed cracking stress intensity factor threshold value be obtained, but also the test cost can be effectively reduced.

CN120706138A.title: Submarine pipeline steel hydrogen-induced fatigue crack propagation prediction method based on hydrogen-induced dislocation pinning-unpinning mechanism
CN120706138A.abstract: The invention discloses a subsea pipeline steel hydrogen-induced fatigue crack propagation prediction method based on a hydrogen-induced dislocation pinning-unpinning mechanism, and the method comprises the steps: constructing a four-stage nonlinear constitutive model of a pipeline steel material in a given hydrogen environment; the four-stage nonlinear constitutive model comprises constitutive relation equations of different stages, which are respectively constructed in four stages of pinning hardening, platform, nail removal and post-nail-removal hardening based on elastic-plastic mechanical behaviors of the pipeline steel material; and endowing the pipeline steel material in the constructed finite element model with the four-stage nonlinear constitutive model, and carrying out pipeline steel hydrogen-induced fatigue crack propagation prediction. According to the method, the staged nonlinear elastic-plastic constitutive model is constructed on the basis of the mechanical property change of the pipeline steel in the hydrogen environment due to hydrogen-induced dislocation pinning and nail disengaging, and the model is used for predicting the hydrogen-induced fatigue crack propagation of the subsea pipeline steel, so that the prediction precision of the crack propagation behavior of the material in the hydrogen environment is remarkably improved.

CN120817859A.title: Multiphase preparation method of aniline derivative
CN120817859A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of fine chemicals and related chemistry, and provides a heterogeneous preparation method of an aniline derivative, which comprises the following steps: taking a nitrobenzene derivative as a raw material, and reacting in an organic solvent at 60-100 DEG C for 24 hours in a hydrogen environment under the action of a high-entropy alloy catalyst to obtain the corresponding aniline derivative. The aniline derivative prepared by the invention has a wide range of applications, and has an extremely important position in drug design and drug synthesis.

CN120668564A.title: Test method for balancing pressure and reducing friction in high-pressure hydrogen environment box
CN120668564A.abstract: The invention discloses a high-pressure hydrogen environment box and a test method for balancing pressure and reducing friction. The environment box comprises a test box body and a gas chamber, a sealing cavity is formed in the test box body; the air chamber is arranged on the test box body in a penetrating mode to form an upper air chamber and a lower air chamber, the upper air chamber and the lower air chamber are communicated with the internal sealing cavity, an upper piston rod is fixedly installed in the upper air chamber, and a lower piston rod is arranged in the lower air chamber in a sliding mode. The upper piston rod and the lower piston rod are respectively sealed with the upper air chamber and the lower air chamber through sealing structures, and the upper piston rod and the lower piston rod are used for mounting a test piece and an extensometer; the invention aims to solve the problems that when a high-pressure hydrogen environment fatigue device in the prior art carries out a fatigue test, an environment box and a piston rod are sealed only by using a sealing ring, so that air leakage is easily caused, and if multiple sealing rings are used, internal and external pressure difference is caused, so that friction force is increased, and test data are influenced.

CN120801161A.abstract: The invention relates to intelligent agent test equipment for intelligently and efficiently evaluating the fatigue crack growth rate of hydrogen pipeline steel and a use method of the intelligent agent test equipment. The problem that the fatigue crack growth rate test efficiency of a material is low under the gaseous hydrogen environment and the fatigue coupling effect is solved. The equipment mainly comprises a high-purity N2 gas cylinder, a high-purity H2 gas cylinder, a hydrogen-doped gas cylinder I, a hydrogen-doped gas cylinder II, an electromagnetic valve I, an electromagnetic valve II, an electromagnetic valve III, an electromagnetic valve IV, a gas inlet pipeline, a vacuum pump, a vacuum gauge, a pressure gauge, a high-pressure hydrogen environment fatigue crack growth rate performance test system and an artificial intelligence decision and control system. Multi-field coupling conditions such as stress amplitude, frequency, pressure and hydrogen doping ratio can be integrated, and the crack propagation behavior of a pipeline steel material in an actual hydrogen service environment can be simulated. A direct-current potential drop device is used for monitoring the fatigue crack propagation state in real time, an artificial intelligence model is embedded at the same time, and load, environment and crack data collected in real time are subjected to online analysis.

CN120662812A.title: Sintering method for alternately strengthening oxygen reduction in vacuum and hydrogen environments
CN120662812A.abstract: The invention relates to a sintering method for alternately strengthening oxygen reduction in a vacuum and hydrogen environment, which comprises the following steps: uniformly doping organic carbon into a metal powder raw material, utilizing the characteristics of high-temperature volatilization and decomposition of the organic carbon, heating the organic carbon to volatilize or decompose the organic carbon into CO, CO2, H2O and other gases in a presintering stage, and forming through open pores in a metal blank after overflowing. The pore structure provides a channel penetrating into the green body for hydrogen, so that the hydrogen can be directly subjected to reduction reaction with metal oxide in the green body, and the problem that the oxide in the green body is difficult to fully reduce due to limited mass transfer in the traditional process is effectively solved. In the reduction process, vacuum hydrogen circulation deoxidation can be carried out for multiple times according to the requirement of a sintered product on oxygen element; in the high-temperature sintering process, a vacuum dehydrogenation method can be carried out according to the requirement of the sintered product on the hydrogen element, the oxygen and hydrogen element content of the final product is reduced, and the comprehensive performance of the sintered product is improved.

CN120727124A.title: Mode-adjustable fracture phase field simulation method considering hydrogen concentration driving mechanism
CN120727124A.abstract: The invention relates to the field of hydrogen induced fracture, and particularly discloses a mode-adjustable fracture phase field simulation method considering a hydrogen concentration driving mechanism, and the method comprises the steps: obtaining material parameters of a test piece; obtaining constitutive equations of stress, phase field and chemical potential through a non-equilibrium state energy dissipation equation; obtaining evolution equations and boundary conditions of the mechanical field, the phase field and the chemical field; establishing a hydrogen-assisted fracture phase field model; establishing a hydrogen embrittlement fracture phase field model with an adjustable mode; obtaining the change relation of the initial deflection angle of the crack along with the weight coefficient; and obtaining results of stress distribution, phase field variables, crack initiation and expansion evolution. Effective regulation and control of the initial deflection angle of the mixed type crack in the hydrogen environment are achieved, comparison verification is conducted on the initial deflection angle and test data, meanwhile, relative contribution of ball quantity and deviator crack driving energy is considered, accurate evaluation of the initiation and expansion path of the mixed type crack is achieved, and the method is suitable for popularization and application. Therefore, an effective method is provided for accurate prediction and mechanism explanation of complex hydrogen embrittlement fracture behaviors.

CN120684817A.abstract: The invention discloses an alpine region hydrogen-containing comprehensive energy system applying a vortex tube and a dispatching method. CN120647510A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of organic synthesis, and particularly discloses a synthetic method of 2-methyl-3-isopropyl phenol. The invention relates to a synthetic method of 2-methyl-3-isopropyl phenol. The synthetic method comprises the following steps: protecting phenolic hydroxyl in 3-bromo-2-methylphenol by using a protecting group to prepare the 2-methyl-3-isopropyl phenol, mixing the intermediate B, a palladium catalyst, isopropenyl boric acid pinacoline ester, a second base catalyst and a polar solvent, and performing Suzuki reaction in a nitrogen environment to obtain an intermediate C; and mixing the intermediate C, palladium on carbon and a protonic solvent, and carrying out hydrogenation reaction in a hydrogen environment to obtain a target product D. The synthesis method disclosed by the invention is high in product yield, mature in process, environment-friendly, safe and suitable for large-scale production, and the application range of the 2-methyl-3-isopropyl phenol can be effectively expanded.

CN223307961U.title: Experiment table for testing high-temperature and high-pressure hydrogen environment
CN223307961U.abstract: The utility model relates to the technical field of experiment machines, in particular to a high-temperature and high-pressure hydrogen environment test experiment table, which not only collects hydrogen after an experiment is completed, facilitates subsequent continuous utilization and improves the utilization rate of the hydrogen, but also performs multiple protection measures on the device, avoids hydrogen leakage and improves the safety of the device. Comprising an experiment table; the device further comprises a protection mechanism, a sealing mechanism, an experiment mechanism, an inflation mechanism and a gas storage mechanism, the protection mechanism is installed on the experiment table and avoids explosion caused by hydrogen leakage, the sealing mechanism is installed on the experiment table and avoids hydrogen leakage, and the experiment mechanism is installed on the experiment table and conducts a clamping test on a workpiece. The inflation mechanism is installed on the experiment table and heats and conveys hydrogen into the experiment table, and the gas storage mechanism is installed on the inflation mechanism and pumps out and stores the hydrogen after an experiment is completed.

CN223332764U.title: Device for testing compatibility of metal material and hydrogen environment
CN223332764U.abstract: The utility model relates to the technical field of metal mechanical property detection testing equipment, in particular to a metal material and hydrogen environment compatibility testing device which comprises an upper clamp, a vacuum pump and a first gas storage bottle, the first gas storage bottle is used for storing high-pressure gas of types required by testing, one end of the upper clamp is detachably connected with a sample, and the other end of the upper clamp is detachably connected with a second gas storage bottle. The upper clamp is used for blocking a central channel of a sample and forming a first cavity body, the vacuum pump and the first gas storage bottle are respectively communicated with the first cavity body, and during use, the upper clamp can be detached from one end of the sample, and the sealed first cavity body is formed between the upper clamp and the central channel of the sample; the second cavity cools the sample to a required temperature, or the sample is heated to the required temperature through the induction heating coil, so that the temperature control during the test of the sample is realized, the test requirement is met, the test accuracy is improved, the test cost is reduced, and the test safety is guaranteed.

CN120593207A.title: Hydrogen pipeline leakage rate detection method, valve and system
CN120593207A.abstract: According to the hydrogen pipeline leakage rate detection method, the valve and the system, the hydrogen pipeline is detected by using a program, a hydrogen leakage point can be quickly positioned, the manual detection time is shortened, meanwhile, an operator can be prevented from being located in a hydrogen environment for a long time, the potential safety hazard is reduced, and the detection efficiency is improved. If a plurality of elbows, tee joints, joints and other connecting pieces are arranged in the pipeline of the detection system, the gas flowability is poor, and the time can be properly increased; if the pipeline of the detection system is basically a straight pipe, the time can be properly shortened, the flexibility of the system can be effectively improved, and personalized setting is carried out according to system application scenes so as to improve the detection efficiency.

CN120609725A.title: Test structure for hydrogen permeation test in high-pressure gas-phase hydrogen environment as well as mounting method and application of test structure
CN120609725A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of hydrogen energy conveying and material performance testing, in particular to a testing structure for hydrogen permeation testing in a high-pressure gas-phase hydrogen environment and an installation method and application of the testing structure. The testing structure comprises a sample body and an insulation sealing assembly, and the sample body is of a double-step disc structure; the uppermost edge of the first sealing insulation assembly is flush with the upper end face of the cylindrical platform, the sample body is installed on the inner side of the first sealing insulation assembly in a matched mode, the second sealing insulation assembly is in a shape with an upward opening, and the first sealing insulation assembly is installed in the second sealing insulation assembly in a matched mode. The test surface of the sample body of the test structure and the gas main flow keep a consistent flow velocity field, and when the test structure is used for testing, the permeation behavior of the hydrogen flow on the material surface can be truly reflected; all the clamping and conducting structures are arranged outside the pipe wall of the test pipeline or at the left part of the sample body, so that flow field disturbance is prevented from being introduced into a main airflow path.

CN120555884A.title: L415MH pipeline steel and preparation method thereof
CN120555884A.abstract: The invention discloses L415MH pipeline steel and a preparation method thereof. The steel meets any of the following items: in a NACE TM0284 standard A solution environment, CLR is less than or equal to 10%, CTR is less than or equal to 3%, and CSR is less than or equal to 1%; a stress corrosion test is carried out according to the NACE TM0177 standard, the loading stress is 0.8 sigma s, and the surface of a tensile sample has no crack; in a 6.3 MPa pure hydrogen environment, the tensile strength, the ductility and the percentage reduction of area of the smooth sample are respectively greater than or equal to 90%, 80% and 75% in a nitrogen environment; in a 6.3 MPa pure hydrogen environment, K1C of the step type compact tensile sample is greater than or equal to 100 MPa.m < 1/2 >. According to the preparation method, in temperature-controlled water cooling after hot rolling, the initial cooling temperature is Ar3-50 DEG C to Ar3-30 DEG C, and the final cooling temperature is Min (TVC, TVN)-300 DEG C to Min (TVC, TVN)-270 DEG C.

CN120555882A.title: Hydrogen-corrosion-resistant steel plate for pipeline and production method thereof
CN120555882A.abstract: The invention discloses a hydrogen-corrosion-resistant steel plate for a pipeline and a production method of the hydrogen-corrosion-resistant steel plate. The steel plate meets at least one of the following conditions: in a NACE TM0284 standard A solution environment, CLR is less than or equal to 10%, CTR is less than or equal to 3%, and CSR is less than or equal to 1%; in a NACE TM0177 standard stress corrosion test, the loading stress is 0.8 sigma s, and the surface of a tensile sample has no crack; in a 6.3 MPa pure hydrogen environment, the tensile strength, the ductility and the percentage reduction of area of the smooth sample are not lower than 90%, 85% and 80% in a nitrogen environment respectively; in a 6.3 MPa pure hydrogen environment, K1C of the step type compact tensile sample is greater than or equal to 110 MPa.m < 1/2 >. The production method comprises the steps of continuous casting, hot rolling and temperature control water cooling. And the initial cooling temperature of temperature control water cooling is Ar3-70 DEG C to Ar3-50 DEG C, and the final cooling temperature is Min (TVC, TVN)-50 DEG C to Min (TVC, TVN)-30 DEG C.

CN120505566A.title: Steel for 450MPa-grade hydrogen-doped conveying pipeline and production method thereof
CN120505566A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of pipeline steel production, in particular to 450MPa-grade hydrogen-doped conveying pipeline steel and a production method thereof. The steel comprises the following chemical components in percentage by weight: 0.051%-0.059% of C, 0.10%-0.20% of Si, 0.70%-0.80% of Mn, 0.016%-0.026% of Nb, 0.013%-0.019% of Ti, 0.20%-0.30% of Ni, 0.12%-0.18% of Mo, less than or equal to 0.012% of P, less than or equal to 0.0015% of S, less than or equal to 0.0040% of N and the balance of Fe and impurities. A low-carbon, low-manganese and low-silicon component design is adopted, a pure steel smelting technology is combined, Nb and Ti are comprehensively utilized for microalloying, Mo is added to improve the volume fraction of a precipitated phase, Ni is added to promote dislocation slippage, and the finished steel plate is good in various properties in a hydrogen environment.

JP2025117356A.title: HYDROGEN SHIELDING FILM
JP2025117356A.abstract: To provide a hydrogen shielding film that can suppress hydrogen embrittlement of a structural material for a long term under a hydrogen environment.SOLUTION: A hydrogen shielding film is constituted of a multilayer film laminated on an outside surface of a structural material comprising a metal and/or an alloy which are susceptible to hydrogen embrittlement. The number of layers constituting the multilayer film is at least two. The number of layers constituting the multilayer film laminated in part of the region of the outside surface of the structural material is different from the number of layers constituting the multilayer film laminated in the other regions of the outside surface of the structural material. In a hydrogen-resistant structural material, the whole outside surface of the structural material is coated on the hydrogen shielding film.SELECTED DRAWING: Figure 1

CN120555479A.title: Construction method and application of heat-resistant yeast for synthesizing mannitol
CN120555479A.abstract: The invention relates to heat-resistant yeast for synthesizing mannitol as well as a construction method and application of the heat-resistant yeast. According to the invention, firstly, Kluyveromyces marxianus is used as a chassis to screen five kinds of mannitol synthesis related genes from different sources, wherein the mannitol yield of the strain is the highest when CmMDH is over-expressed. And then, promoting the expression of the NADPH synthetic gene by increasing the copy number of the CmMDH and overexpression, so as to obtain the strain Y2CmMDH-KmUTR1 with higher yield. The production of the mannitol is further promoted by subsequently improving the oxygen introduction and pH (Potential of Hydrogen) environment. According to the invention, the yeast strain for efficiently producing the mannitol at 42 DEG C is constructed for the first time, and the mannitol is produced by using the molasses and the jerusalem artichoke without adding extra enzymes. The method has important significance for realizing industrial production of mannitol and recycling of agricultural wastes through a microbial synthesis method.

CN120385577A.title: Low-temperature high-pressure hydrogen environment mechanical test bed
CN120385577A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of test devices, and provides a low-temperature high-pressure hydrogen environment mechanical test bench, which comprises a hydrogen environment device, a refrigeration device and a test device, and is characterized in that the hydrogen environment device comprises a hydrogen environment box and a hydrogen supply mechanism, and the hydrogen supply mechanism supplies hydrogen to the hydrogen environment box; the refrigerating device refrigerates the hydrogen environment box; the testing device comprises a testing clamp, a transmission rod and a driving mechanism, the transmission rod is arranged in the hydrogen environment box in a reciprocating sliding mode, the first end of the transmission rod extends into the hydrogen environment box, the second end of the transmission rod extends out of the hydrogen environment box, the testing clamp comprises a first clamping part and a second clamping part, and the first clamping part is fixed relative to the hydrogen environment box; the second clamping part is connected with the first end of the transmission rod. CN120398110A.abstract: The invention provides a preparation method of TiO2 in a mixed crystal form, TiO2 loaded with elemental palladium as well as a preparation method and application of the TiO2, and belongs to the field of hydrogen sensing. The invention provides a preparation method of TiO2 in a mixed crystal form, which comprises the following steps: carrying out annealing treatment on TiO2 in a single crystal form to obtain TiO2 in the mixed crystal form; the annealing treatment is carried out in an atmosphere containing CO. The crystal form and the surface property of the TiO2 can be changed in the process of annealing the single crystal form TiO2 in the CO-containing atmosphere to form the mixed crystal form TiO2, so that the hydrogen adsorption capacity and the response speed of the TiO2 are improved. Furthermore, by loading Pd, the sensitivity and high selectivity of the catalyst to hydrogen can be further enhanced, and meanwhile, the catalyst also has relatively high detection performance in a low-concentration hydrogen environment and at a low working temperature.

CN120369457A.title: Method and device for detecting hydrogen embrittlement resistance of high-entropy alloy based on machine learning driving
CN120369457A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of high-entropy alloy hydrogen embrittlement resistance detection, and discloses a high-entropy alloy hydrogen embrittlement resistance detection method and device based on machine learning driving, and the method comprises the steps of calculation of elongation at break, setting of an elongation at break threshold and comparison of the elongation at break and the threshold. According to the method, the brittleness resistance of the high-entropy alloy in the hydrogen environment is evaluated through the unified standard elongation at break, and the detection reliability and comparability can be ensured regardless of the sample size. Compared with the direct comparison of the fracture length, the fracture elongation is not influenced by the sample size, so that the samples with different sizes can be evaluated under the unified standard, and the error caused by the size difference of the samples is avoided.

CN120369790A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of hydrogen behavior tests in metal materials, and particularly relates to a high-temperature electrochemical hydrogen charging method suitable for 316L stainless steel. CN120383479A.title: High-strength boron carbide composite material and preparation method thereof
CN120383479A.abstract: The invention belongs to the technical field of composite materials, and particularly relates to a high-strength boron carbide composite material and a preparation method thereof.The preparation method comprises the following steps that S1, modification of titanium boride whiskers is conducted, specifically, the titanium boride whiskers, silicon powder and a grinding aid are put into a ball milling tank, ball milling, cleaning and drying are conducted, and the modified titanium boride whiskers are obtained; s2, preparation of composite material powder: mixing phenolic resin, boron carbide powder and deionized water, performing primary ball milling, then adding the modified titanium boride whisker, performing secondary ball milling, drying, and performing tertiary ball milling to obtain the composite material powder; and S3, sintering of the composite material: pressing the powder into blocks, drying moisture, pyrolyzing in a hydrogen environment, then coating with silicon powder, and sintering in vacuum. Titanium boride whiskers are adopted as a toughening and reinforcing component to reinforce and toughen the boron carbide composite material, and a liquid phase siliconizing sintering method is adopted to immerse the silicon element into the composite material system, so that the composite material is obtained. And silicon carbide can be generated in situ in the composite material, so that composite toughening of titanium boride and silicon carbide is realized.

CN120329027A.title: Titanium zirconium oxide spraying powder, titanium zirconium oxide target material and preparation method of titanium zirconium oxide target material
CN120329027A.abstract: The invention discloses titanium zirconium oxide spraying powder, a titanium zirconium oxide target material and a preparation method of the titanium zirconium oxide spraying powder and the titanium zirconium oxide target material, and belongs to the technical field of plasma powder preparation. The titanium zirconium oxide spraying powder provided by the invention is prepared by the following steps: mixing zirconium oxide and titanium oxide according to a certain mass ratio, presintering, crushing, mixing the crushed titanium zirconium oxide and water according to a certain mass ratio, grinding, sintering in a vacuum environment or a hydrogen environment, crushing and screening after sintering, and thus obtaining the titanium zirconium oxide spraying powder. Meanwhile, no other metal powder or binder is added in the preparation process of the titanium zirconium oxide spraying powder provided by the invention, so that the preparation cost can be effectively reduced, and the production efficiency can be improved. Furthermore, when the titanium zirconium oxide spraying powder provided by the invention is used for preparing a titanium zirconium oxide target material, the obtained titanium zirconium oxide target material is high in density and low in resistivity.

CN120333725A.abstract: The invention provides an air tightness detection device and method for a metal hose in a hydrogen environment, and relates to the technical field of hose detection equipment. The device comprises a metal hose, the two ends of the metal hose are sealed, hydrogen with preset pressure is contained in the metal hose, and the fixed end of the metal hose is fixedly arranged; the vertical moving assembly comprises a vertical sliding rail and a pair of vertical sliding tables arranged on the vertical sliding rail in a sliding mode. One end of each telescopic piece is connected with the vertical sliding table; three end parts of the connecting piece are respectively hinged with the pair of telescopic pieces and the swinging end of the metal hose; and the detection end of the pressure detection piece is arranged in the metal hose. Hydrogen is contained in the metal hose, one end of the metal hose is fixed, the swing end of the metal hose is moved up and down through the vertical moving assembly to drive the swing end to bend downwards and upwards, then airtightness detection of the metal hose filled with hydrogen is achieved, and dynamic swing of the metal hose in the actual working condition is simulated.

CN120340981A.title: Rare earth permanent magnet material composition design for extreme environment
CN120340981A.abstract: The invention discloses component design of a rare earth permanent magnet material for an extreme environment, and belongs to the field of rare earth permanent magnet materials. According to the invention, Sm, Co and Cu elementary substances are used as raw materials, and samarium cobalt-based rare earth permanent magnet material powder with Cu-doped chemical components is prepared through electric arc melting and physical crushing processes. The samarium-cobalt-based permanent magnet material has good hydrogen resistance, and the problem that the SmCo5 alloy has high hydrogen absorption capacity in a hydrogen environment is solved. According to the method, the hydrogen resistance of the SmCo5-based permanent magnet can be effectively improved, the comprehensive magnetic performance of the SmCo5-based permanent magnet is also improved, and meanwhile, the cost is reduced by replacing part of Co with Cu. The preparation can be completed by using conventional equipment, the process is simple, and a new feasible method is provided for the development of the hydrogen-resistant SmCo5 magnet.

CN120257731A.title: Method for analyzing sealing performance of ultralow-temperature aviation liquid hydrogen floating ball valve
CN120257731A.abstract: The invention discloses a sealing performance analysis method for an ultralow-temperature aviation liquid hydrogen floating ball valve. The method comprises the steps that materials of a ball valve seat and a ball body are determined; simplifying the contact between the ball body and the valve seat to obtain a sealing structure model; setting different pressure angles for the sealing structure model to obtain a change result of the path sealing specific pressure of the sealing surface; the optimal pressure angle of the valve seat is determined according to the change result of the path sealing specific pressure of the sealing surface; and based on the optimal pressure angle, the change of the sealing specific pressure under different sealing surface widths is analyzed, and the optimal sealing surface width of the valve seat is determined. According to the method, the influence of different pressure angles and sealing surface widths on the sealing performance in the ultralow-temperature liquid hydrogen environment can be analyzed, and technical reference is provided for design of the ultralow-temperature aviation liquid hydrogen ball valve.

CN120285965A.title: Preparation and test method of In2O3-rGO-PPy aerogel for rapidly detecting hydrogen at room temperature
CN120285965A.abstract: The invention discloses a preparation method and a test method of In2O3-rGO-PPy aerogel for rapidly detecting hydrogen at room temperature. The preparation method comprises the following specific steps: mixing CO (NH2) 2, In (NO3) 3.4. 5H2O and NH4F, reacting in a reaction kettle, washing and purifying after the reaction is completed, and annealing at 400 DEG C for 1-2 hours to prepare indium oxide (In2O3) nanoparticles; the preparation method comprises the following steps: preparing methyl orange, FeCl3. 6H2O and pyrrole into a mixed solution, standing to obtain a product, washing and purifying, and finally freeze-drying to obtain a polypyrrole (PPy) tube; the preparation method comprises the following steps: dissolving graphene oxide, polypyrrole and indium oxide in deionized water, carrying out ultrasonic treatment in an ice bath, carrying out a hydrothermal reaction to obtain composite hydrogel, washing and purifying the composite hydrogel, and finally carrying out freeze drying to obtain the three-dimensional In2O3-rGO-PPy aerogel. The innovative overall testing method is used for testing the sensor, a metal sheet and a wire are connected to the two ends of aerogel, the aerogel is integrally placed in a gas chamber, the introduction amount of hydrogen and air is controlled, a hydrogen environment with a certain concentration is kept, and the sensor is tested under the condition.

CN120213747A.title: Hydrogen compatibility simulation test method under high-pressure environment based on hydrogen diffusion
CN120213747A.abstract: The invention discloses a hydrogen compatibility simulation test method in a high-pressure environment based on hydrogen diffusion. The method comprises the following steps: S1, preparing a sample; s2, performing a hydrogen permeation test in a high-pressure hydrogen environment; s3, preparing a hydrogen permeation cathode hydrogen filling solution; s4, performing a hydrogen permeation test in a simulated high-pressure hydrogen environment; s5, applying and adjusting cathode hydrogen charging current under simulated high-pressure hydrogen permeation; s6, determining a dynamic hydrogen charging current; s7, performing a smooth slow tensile simulation test in a high-pressure hydrogen environment; s8, carrying out a notch slow tensile simulation test in a high-pressure hydrogen environment; s9, carrying out a fracture toughness test; and S10, performing high-pressure hydrogen embrittlement evaluation based on the results of the smooth slow tensile simulation test and the fracture toughness test. According to the method, the hydrogen embrittlement performance of the material under different hydrogen pressure environments can be obtained, and the influence of the influence factors on the high-pressure hydrogen embrittlement performance of the steel can be analyzed in combination with the process, alloy components and stress states of the steel.

CN120213746A.abstract: The invention discloses a metal material hydrogen compatibility simulation test method based on hydrogen content. CN120138483A.title: Hydrogen embrittlement precipitation resistant strengthened high-nickel austenitic stainless steel and preparation method thereof
CN120138483A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of preparation of novel hydrogen embrittlement-resistant materials of high-strength austenitic stainless steel, in particular to hydrogen embrittlement precipitation-resistant reinforced high-nickel austenitic stainless steel and a preparation method thereof. The high-nickel austenitic stainless steel is subjected to high-temperature solution treatment, so that solute elements of the high-nickel austenitic stainless steel are uniformly dissolved in an austenite matrix, then rolling treatment is performed, and a uniform and fine austenite structure is obtained; then, medium and low temperature tempering treatment is carried out to obtain a uniformly-distributed double-phase structure, and a high-temperature medium-rate rolling deformation process is adopted to obtain an ultra-fine grain structure; and finally, a nano precipitated phase is formed under the low-temperature condition, the strength of an austenite structure is improved, the hydrogen embrittlement resistance is enhanced, the austenite structure is not coarsened after welding, and therefore the use requirement in the low-temperature hydrogen environment with the high pressure of 70 Mpa is met. The problem that austenitic stainless steel in the prior art is poor in hydrogen embrittlement resistance and cannot meet application in a high-pressure and low-temperature hydrogen environment is solved.

CN120170320A.abstract: The high-entropy alloy flux-cored wire comprises a flux core and a welding skin, the flux core is composed of, by mass, 5%-10% of Co, 10%-20% of Cr, 10%-15% of Ni, 20%-30% of Al, 15%-35% of Mn and 1%-5% of La2O3, and the sum of the mass percentages of the components is 100%. CN120176888A.title: Load sensor structure resistant to high-pressure hydrogen environment
CN120176888A.abstract: The invention belongs to the field of design and manufacture of high-pressure hydrogen storage containers, and discloses a load sensor resistant to influence of a high-pressure hydrogen environment, which adopts a linear variable differential transformer (LVDT) to replace a strain gauge to serve as a sensitive element for measuring deformation, adopts a material resistant to hydrogen embrittlement to manufacture a sensor elastic body, and connects the LVDT with the elastic body through a thread and tenon structure. According to the invention, the problem of signal drift of a current load sensor in a high-pressure hydrogen environment is effectively solved, and the problem of inaccurate test results of basic mechanical properties of a material in the hydrogen environment is solved.

CN120012464A.title: Phase field simulation method, device and equipment for simulating hydrogen-induced fatigue crack propagation of material and storage medium
CN120012464A.abstract: The invention relates to the technical field of computational material science, and discloses a phase field simulation method, device and equipment for simulating hydrogen-induced fatigue crack propagation of a material and a storage medium. The method comprises the following steps: acquiring hydrogen transmission parameters and mechanical property parameters of a target material, and establishing a material deformation equation, a hydrogen diffusion equation and a phase field evolution equation corresponding to the target material based on the hydrogen transmission parameters and the mechanical property parameters; constructing a three-field coupling relation by using a material deformation equation, a hydrogen diffusion equation and a phase field evolution equation to obtain a three-field coupling phase field simulation model; and building boundary conditions of a hydrogen diffusion equation based on a surface hydrogen absorption flux equation corresponding to the target material, and solving the three-field coupling phase field simulation model to obtain crack propagation parameters of the target material in the gaseous hydrogen environment. Therefore, through the constructed three-field coupling phase field simulation model, the dynamic crack propagation behavior of the material in the gaseous hydrogen environment is accurately described, and the prediction precision of the crack propagation process of the material in the complex environment is remarkably improved.

CN119932446A.title: Stainless steel suitable for high-pressure hydrogen environment
CN119932446A.abstract: The stainless steel material suitable for the high-pressure hydrogen environment comprises the following components in percentage by weight: 16 to 22 weight percent of chromium (Cr), 7 to 9 weight percent of nickel (Ni), 10 to 13 weight percent of manganese (Mn), 0.1 to 0.3 weight percent of niobium (Nb), 0.1 to 0.3 weight percent of vanadium (V), 0.4 to 0.5 weight percent of nitrogen (N) and the balance of necessary trace elements and inevitable impurities, and the nickel equivalent (Nieq) of the components of the stainless steel material is between 35 and 42. By greatly increasing the manganese content of the composition of the stainless steel material, the nitrogen content is synchronously increased to achieve solution strengthening and stabilize the austenite phase of the stainless steel material, in addition, the stainless steel material can generate an MX precipitated phase in the stretching process by adding niobium and vanadium at the same time, the hydrogen embrittlement resistance of the stainless steel material can be improved, and the stainless steel material can have high tensile strength.

CN120026357A.title: Gamma-manganese oxide doped carbon layer coated Pt and Co catalyst as well as preparation method and application thereof
CN120026357A.abstract: The invention discloses a gamma-manganese oxide doped carbon layer coated Pt and Co catalyst as well as a preparation method and application thereof, and belongs to the technical field of biomass resource utilization. The method comprises the following steps: putting gamma-MnO2, glucose, carbon black, chloroplatinic acid hexahydrate and/or cobalt nitrate hexahydrate into a reaction container, adding deionized water, heating, stirring, reacting, drying, calcining in a nitrogen environment, and calcining in a hydrogen environment to obtain the gamma-manganese oxide doped carbon layer coated Pt and Co catalyst. The catalyst can be used for preparing a monocyclic product through lignin depolymerization, so that the conversion rate of lignin reaches 100%, the yield of the monocyclic product reaches 94%, and the Faraday efficiency reaches 84%. After the catalyst is recycled for 20 times, the conversion rate and the Faraday efficiency are not attenuated.

CN119909606A.title: Oxygen carrier lifting circulation system for hydrogen production through chemical looping reaction
CN119909606A.abstract: The invention relates to an oxygen carrier lifting circulation system for hydrogen production through chemical looping reaction. The oxygen carrier lifting circulation system comprises an oxygen carrier feeding hopper, an oxygen environment reaction unit, a nitrogen sealing tank, a hydrogen environment reaction unit and an oxygen carrier lifting unit which are sequentially arranged. An oxygen carrier flows among the oxygen environment reaction unit, the nitrogen sealing tank and the hydrogen environment reaction unit in sequence from the oxygen carrier feeding hopper through self gravity flow until the oxygen carrier reaches the oxygen carrier lifting unit; the device further comprises a lifting gas circulation unit, the lifting gas circulation unit is used for providing recyclable lifting gas, and the oxygen carrier reaching the oxygen carrier lifting unit is lifted and returned to the oxygen carrier feeding hopper under the driving of the lifting gas; the lifting gas comprises circularly flowing nitrogen, and the nitrogen sealing tank and the lifting gas circulating unit are respectively connected with a nitrogen supplementing unit. According to the invention, switching of the oxygen carrier in different reaction gas environments can be realized, and the oxygen carrier can be stably and reliably circulated in the system.

CN119869514A.title: Ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst and plastic hydrogenolysis method
CN119869514A.abstract: The invention provides a ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst and a plastic hydrogenolysis method. The ruthenium monatomic alloy catalyst comprises ruthenium monatomic alloy and a carrier, the general formula of the ruthenium monatomic alloy is RuSAM, M is selected from one or a combination of more than two of Pt, Pd, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Mg, Ca and Mn; wherein the value of SA ranges from 0.01 to 1. CN119915614A.title: Mobile assembly and disassembly type high-temperature and high-pressure hydrogen environment in-situ tensile test device and method
CN119915614A.abstract: The invention provides a mobile assembly and disassembly type high-temperature and high-pressure hydrogen environment in-situ tensile test device and method, the device is suitable for a universal testing machine, and the device comprises a mobile base frame, a power supporting assembly and an assembly and disassembly type tensile kettle. The base frame is provided with a gravity balance plate and a guide rail, and the supporting frame sliding block slides on the guide rail and is convenient to move. The power supporting assembly comprises a hollow supporting stand column, a lifting sleeve, a mounting arm and a lifting supporting plate, and lifting and rotating of the stretching kettle are achieved. The stretching kettle is composed of a kettle body, a kettle cover and a stretching shaft and can fix a stretching sample, and high-pressure hydrogen is prevented from leaking in a combined sealing mode. The method comprises the steps of sample installation, gas inflation and deflation, heating, alignment, tensile test, data storage, cooling disassembly and the like. The problems that a traditional device is poor in flexibility, limited in performance, inaccurate in strain measurement and the like are solved, the testing efficiency and applicability are improved, and the long-term mechanical behavior of the metal sealing layer in the high-pressure and high-temperature hydrogen environment can be explored easily.

JP2025054905A.abstract: To provide a martensitic stainless steel for high-pressure hydrogen components almost free from strength decrease under high pressure hydrogen environment, excellent in low temperature toughness, extremely excellent also in corrosion resistance, and capable of realizing resource saving compared with a conventional stainless steel material for high-pressure hydrogen components, and a high-pressure hydrogen component therewith.SOLUTION: A martensitic stainless steel for high-pressure hydrogen components has a chemical composition that includes C: 0.01-0.05%, Si: 0.70% or less, Mn: 1.10% or less, P: 0.040% or less, S: 0.030% or less, Ni: 4.00-5.70%, Cr: 17.00-20.50%, Mo: 0.20-2.00%, and, as optional elements, Ca: 0.0005-0.0030%, and B: 0.0005-0.0050%, satisfying the equation 3.0>Ni-0.8Cr+11.36>0 (where the element symbols in equation 1 represent the content percentage (%) of each element), with the balance consisting of Fe and inevitable impurities.SELECTED DRAWING: None

028-645-864-448-73X.title: Laser system
028-645-864-448-73X.abstract: A gas laser system utilizing a mixture of water vapor, hydrogen or hydrogen deuteride and nitrogen wherein molecular vibrations are excited in the hydrogen or hydrogen deuteride in an electric discharge and the vibrational energy is stored until, by means of near-resonant energy exchange collisions, it is transferred to the active laser water molecules. Nitrogen is added to stabilize the discharge and allow coherent emission at 4.6 microns with a theoretical efficiency of about 58%.

034-929-100-331-816.title: Method and apparatus for solid bonding, a conductor bonding method, a packaging method, and a bonding agent and a method for manufacturing a bonding agent
034-929-100-331-816.abstract: A method and apparatus for solid bonding without using a bonding agent are provided. A surface of metal, glass, or other bond members 16a and 16b is fluorinated by exposure to a mixture of HF gas from a HF gas supply unit 24 and water vapor from a vapor generator 26 in a fluorination process section 12. The bond members 16a and 16b are then placed in contact at the fluorinated surface on table 36 in bonding process section 14. Argon is then introduced to bonding chamber 34. Pressure is then applied to the first bond member 16a and second bond member 16b by a cylinder 46, and heated to below the melting point by a heater 48, to bond the first and second bond members together.

022-763-908-473-750.title: MULTISPARK REACTOR
022-763-908-473-750.abstract: A device includes a channel having an inlet and an outlet, a first electrode disposed within the channel, and a second electrode disposed within the channel so as to define a gap between the second electrode and the first electrode. The device further includes a power source connected to at least one of the electrodes. The second electrode includes a lumen from a first end of the second electrode to a second end of the second electrode. The lumen is configured to introduce a carrier gas to the gap. The inlet is configured to introduce a process stream to the channel. The process stream comprises a viscous petroleum feed material. The power source is configured to produce a spark within the gap, thereby generating a plasma configured to reduce a viscosity of the viscous petroleum feed material and to form a processed petroleum material.

010-967-851-367-963.title: Device and method for preparing nanoscale active biological calcium particles
010-967-851-367-963.abstract: The invention provides a preparation device and method of nanoscale active biological calcium particles, and relates to the technical field of calcium particle preparation, the preparation device comprises a primary sealing cylinder body, a first spark ignition device, a secondary sealing cylinder body, a second spark ignition device and a drying separator; the first spark ignition device is matched with the first-stage sealing cylinder body, the first spark ignition device ignites and explodes calcium coarse particles, hydrogen and oxygen in the first-stage sealing cylinder body, and a mixture of first-stage fine particles and water vapor is formed; the second spark ignition device is matched with the second-stage sealing cylinder body, the second spark ignition device is used for igniting and blasting the first-stage fine particles, the hydrogen and the oxygen in the second-stage sealing cylinder body, and a mixture of second-stage fine particles and water vapor is formed; the drying separator is used for removing moisture in the secondary fine particles, the water vapor outlet is used for outputting water vapor, the third outlet is used for outputting the secondary fine particles, and the nanoscale active biological calcium particles are prepared.

037-061-809-964-886.title: System for identifying and quantifying selected constituents of gas samples using selective photoionization
037-061-809-964-886.abstract: A spark detection apparatus is set forth and incorporates a closed chamber for receiving a carrier gas flowing therethrough between inlets and outlets, and the carrier gas is exposed to a pair of electrodes forming a spark across the chamber and through the carrier gas. One component of the carrier gas is argon. The sample to be analyzed is injected into the carrier gas. One reaction involves the formation of metastable argon which upon decay serves as a source of ionizing radiation which reacts with sample compounds producing detectable events. These events are used to identify and quantify unknown compounds contained in the sample. The methods and apparatus are especially useful in measuring pollutant compounds in atmospheric samples since the ionizing radiation emitted from the decay of metastable argon is below the ionization potential of the major constituents of air. This greatly enhances the signal-to-noise ratio for detecting impurity compounds when the detection system is used to monitor air quality. Air is not energized.

041-819-611-909-065.title: Desulfurizer and method of desulfurization
041-819-611-909-065.abstract: The present invention provides a desulfurization apparatus 10 for desulfurizing a flue gas 13 containing sulfur oxides through contact with a porous carbon material 11. The carbon material 11, which is provided in a desulfurization tower 12, is at least one species selected from activated carbon and activated carbon fiber. The apparatus 10 contains an NO2-gas-feeding apparatus 14 for feeding NO2 gas into the desulfurization tower 12. Within the desulfurization tower 12, showering means 15 is provided at the top, the showering means adjusting the water content of the flue gas in the desulfurization tower to that corresponding to saturation with water vapor or higher at the treatment temperature.

124-864-388-893-305.title: Process for plasma-chemical conversion of N2 O into NOx
124-864-388-893-305.abstract: PCT No. PCT/FR94/01048 Sec. 371 Date Jul. 14, 1995 Sec. 102(e) Date Jul. 14, 1995 PCT Filed Sep. 6, 1994 PCT Pub. No. WO95/07234 PCT Pub. Date Mar. 16, 1995A process for the conversion of N2O into NOx (x=1 or 2) thereof, comprising forming a jet of gas to be treated comprising N2O and directing the jet into the interstitial space of at least two divergent electrodes placed under an electric voltage, to create at least one electric discharge and/or arc, and is part of a low-temperature plasma generator. The generator works by formation of electric discharges sliding between the electrodes such that the gas circulates along the electrodes from the discharge region where these electrodes are closest to each other. Finally, the NOx gas formed is recovered. The invention also relates to a device for carrying out the process.

114-451-114-848-98X.title: System for generating and transmitting acoustic signals underwater
114-451-114-848-98X.abstract: PCT No. PCT/US96/12404 Sec. 371 Date Jan. 16, 1998 Sec. 102(e) Date Jan. 16, 1998 PCT Filed Jul. 29, 1996 PCT Pub. No. WO97/06452 PCT Pub. Date Feb. 20, 1997An array system, which provides a unipolar acoustic impulse with a broad spectral response underwater, utilizes a vertical array of airgun or electrical spark sources whose individual peak source strengths are essentially constant over the array length, but whose individual source periods vary with depth. The array of sources produces in each instance a near-unipolar, impulsive signature with a broad pedestal-like spectrum. The system may be used for underwater object location and also for seismic exploration.
